item.content:encoded:
Visble - Terms of Use

1. Introduction

Welcome to Visble, an analytics platform for Generative Engine Optimization offered by United Innovation Hub. These Terms of Use (the “Terms”) govern your access to and use of Visble’s websites, applications, and related services (collectively, the “Services”). By creating an account, starting a trial, or using the Services, you agree to be bound by these Terms and our Privacy Policy.

These Terms are intended for business customers (B2B) using the Services for internal business purposes.

 2. Definitions

  • “Customer,” “you,” “your”: the organization or person accepting these Terms.
  • “Authorized Users”: individuals you permit to access the Services under your account.
  • “Customer Content”: data, text, files, inputs, prompts, configurations, and other materials you (or your Users) submit to the Services.
  • “Order Form”: any online selection (checkout) or written document specifying plan, term, and pricing.

3. Accounts & Access

3.1) Registration & Responsibility. You must provide accurate account information and keep it up to date. You are responsible for all activities that occur under your account, including by Authorized Users, and for maintaining the confidentiality of credentials.

3.2) License to Use Services. Subject to your continuous compliance with these Terms and any applicable Order Form, Visble grants you a limited, non‑exclusive, non‑transferable, revocable license to access and use the Services solely for your internal business purposes during the applicable subscription term.

3.3) Customer Content & License. You retain all rights in and to your Customer Content. You grant Visble a worldwide, non‑exclusive, royalty‑free license to host, store, reproduce, process, transmit, and display Customer Content only as necessary to provide and maintain the Services, to prevent or address technical or security issues, and to comply with law. Visble may generate aggregated and de‑identified analytics from usage of the Services (excluding personal data in identifiable form) to improve and operate the Services. Visble does not sell Customer Content.

3.4) Feedback. If you provide feedback or suggestions, you grant Visble a perpetual, irrevocable, worldwide, royalty‑free license to use the feedback without restriction.

3.5) Logo & Name Usage (Opt‑out Available). By subscribing to the Services (including free trials and paid subscriptions), you grant Visble permission to display your company name and logo on Visble’s website and marketing materials (e.g., customer lists, case studies, presentations). Visble will not alter your logo or misrepresent the relationship. You may opt out at any time by emailing info@visble.ai with subject line “Logo Usage Opt‑Out.” Within 30 days of receipt, Visble will remove your logo from active promotional materials.

4. Acceptable Use

You will not (and will not allow any third party to): (a) use the Services for unlawful, infringing, or harmful activities; (b) attempt to gain unauthorized access to the Services or related systems; (c) interfere with or disrupt the integrity or performance of the Services; (d) reverse engineer, decompile, or create derivative works of the Services; (e) use automated means to scrape, harvest, or extract data from the Services except as permitted by written agreement; (f) transmit malware or spam; or (g) use the Services to develop a competing product.

 

5. Free Trial

  • 14‑day free trial with no card required.
  • During trial, you may create campaigns and receive weekly metrics updates.
  • After the trial ends, unless you begin a paid subscription: (i) you can no longer create new campaigns; and (ii) weekly metrics updates will stop. Previous data may remain view‑only for a limited period at Visble’s discretion.

6. Fees, Billing & Taxes

6.1) Plans & Currency. We offer monthly, quarterly, and annual plans. All fees are in USD unless stated otherwise in an Order Form.

6.2) Auto‑Renewal. Subscriptions renew automatically for successive terms equal to the initial term unless cancelled before the renewal date in the account settings or as otherwise specified in an Order Form.

6.3) Payment Methods. Card/online payments are charged in advance at the start of each billing cycle. If an invoice arrangement is agreed in writing, fees are invoiced in advance per the billing cycle and due within 14 days of the invoice date unless otherwise stated.

6.4) Late Payments; Suspension. Overdue amounts may accrue interest at 1.5% per month (18% per annum) or the maximum rate permitted by law, whichever is lower. After notice and a reasonable cure period, Visble may suspend access until amounts are paid. You remain responsible for all accrued charges during suspension.

6.5) Price Changes. We may update prices for future terms with prior notice. Price changes do not affect current prepaid terms.

6.6) Taxes & Withholding. Fees are exclusive of taxes. You are responsible for all GST/VAT, sales, use, duties, and similar taxes arising from your purchases, excluding taxes based on Visble’s net income. If withholding taxes apply, you will gross‑up payments so Visble receives the full amount invoiced, or provide valid exemption or withholding certificates. Where applicable under Indian law, Visble may charge GST and you agree to provide your GSTIN and accurate billing details.

7. Refund and Cancellation Policy

All subscription payments are final and non‑refundable, except in the following limited circumstances:

  1. Duplicate payments or accidental double billing. Duplicate amounts will be refunded.
  2. Proven billing errors by Visble. Incorrect amounts caused by Visble’s processing error will be refunded.
  3. Failure of service delivery. If core Services are unavailable due to a continuous, platform‑wide outage lasting more than 14 consecutive days, you may request a refund for the affected billing period.

 

How to request: Email info@visble.ai within 7 days of the qualifying event. Approved refunds are processed within 10 business days to the original payment method.

8. Service Availability; Maintenance; Beta Features

8.1) Availability. We strive for high availability but do not guarantee uninterrupted operation. Maintenance, updates, or factors outside our control may cause temporary interruptions.

8.2) Beta/Preview. We may offer beta or preview features “as‑is,” without support or commitments, and they may be modified or discontinued at any time.

9. Data Protection & Security

9.1) Privacy. Processing of personal data is described in our Privacy Policy.

9.2) Security. We implement commercially reasonable technical and organizational measures to protect Customer Content. You are responsible for securing your accounts, endpoints, and any local environments.

9.3) Data Export & Retention. During the subscription term, you may export Customer Content using available tools. Following termination, we may retain Customer Content for up to 30 days for limited recovery, after which it may be deleted, except as required by law or agreed otherwise in writing.

10. Confidentiality

Each party may access the other’s non‑public information marked confidential or reasonably understood as confidential (“Confidential Information”). A receiving party will use Confidential Information only to perform under these Terms, protect it using reasonable measures, and not disclose it except to employees, contractors, and advisors who need to know and are bound by confidentiality obligations. Exclusions include information that is public, already known, independently developed, or lawfully obtained from a third party. A party may disclose Confidential Information when legally compelled, with notice where lawful.

11. Intellectual Property; Reservation of Rights

Except for the limited rights expressly granted, Visble and its licensors retain all rights, title, and interest in and to the Services, software, designs, and documentation. No other licenses are granted by implication or otherwise. 

12. Warranties & Disclaimers

THE SERVICES ARE PROVIDED “AS IS” AND “AS AVAILABLE.” TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, VISIBLE DISCLAIMS ALL WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, AND NON‑INFRINGEMENT. Outputs generated by or through the Services (including AI‑assisted insights) are probabilistic and may contain errors; you remain solely responsible for decisions made based on such outputs.

13. Limitation of Liability

TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, NEITHER PARTY WILL BE LIABLE FOR INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, EXEMPLARY, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES (INCLUDING LOST PROFITS, REVENUE, OR DATA), EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY. EXCEPT FOR (A) YOUR PAYMENT OBLIGATIONS; (B) YOUR BREACH OF SECTION 4 (ACCEPTABLE USE) OR IP INFRINGEMENT; OR (C) A PARTY’S WILLFUL MISCONDUCT OR BODILY INJURY, EACH PARTY’S TOTAL AGGREGATE LIABILITY ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THE SERVICES WILL NOT EXCEED THE AMOUNTS PAID BY YOU TO VISBLE IN THE 12 MONTHS PRECEDING THE EVENT GIVING RISE TO THE CLAIM. 

14. Indemnification

You will indemnify and hold harmless Visble, its affiliates, and personnel from and against claims, damages, losses, liabilities, costs, and expenses (including reasonable legal fees) arising out of (a) your Customer Content; (b) your use of the Services in violation of these Terms or law; or (c) your infringement or misappropriation of third‑party rights.

15. Term; Suspension; Termination

15.1) Term. These Terms take effect when you first accept them and continue while you use the Services.

15.2) Suspension. We may suspend access immediately if we reasonably believe there is a security risk, unlawful activity, or material breach, or for non‑payment after notice.

15.3) Termination. Either party may terminate for material breach that remains uncured 7 days after written notice. You may terminate at any time via your account; termination takes effect at the end of the then‑current paid term unless otherwise required by law or stated in an Order Form.

15.4) Effect of Termination. Upon termination or expiration, your right to access the Services ends. Sections intended to survive (including **Fees & Taxes, Refund Policy, IP, Confident)

16. Limitation of Liability

To the maximum extent permitted by law, Visble’s liability is limited to the amount you paid in the past 12 months. We are not liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages.

17. Governing Law

These Terms are governed by and construed under the laws of India, with exclusive jurisdiction of courts in New Delhi, India

18. Changes to Terms

We may update these Terms periodically. Continued use of the Services after changes indicates your acceptance of the updated Terms.

19. Contact Information

For legal or support inquiries:

United Innovation Hub (Visble)  

Wework, Two Horizon Centre, Gurugram, Haryana-122009

Email: info@visble.ai

18. Changes to Terms

We may update these Terms periodically. Continued use of the Services after changes indicates your acceptance of the updated Terms.

19. Contact Information

For legal or support inquiries:

United Innovation Hub (Visble)  

Wework, Two Horizon Centre, Gurugram, Haryana-122009

Email: info@visble.ai

item.title: Privacy Policy
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Visble - Privacy Policy

Last updated: 6 September 2025

1. Introduction

This Privacy Policy describes how Visble, operated by United Innovation Hub, collects, uses, discloses, and protects personal information when you use our Services. By accessing or using Visble, you agree to this Policy.

2. Definitions

Personal Data: Any information that can identify you, such as email, name, or IP address.
Usage Data: Automatically collected information like device data, pages visited, time stamps, etc.
Services: Visble’s websites, web apps, and related services for Generative Engine Optimization analytics.

3. Information We Collect

Data You Provide: account registration, communications, payment details, profile preferences.
Automatically Collected Data: usage data, device info, cookies.
From Third-Party Services: integrations such as Google Analytics, with your permission.

4. How We Use Your Data

To provide and enhance services, manage user authentication, respond to support, analyze usage, and send important updates.

5. Sharing & Disclosure

Service Providers, Affiliates, Business Transfers, and Legal Compliance, with safeguards.

6. Data Retention

We retain data as long as needed to provide Services and comply with laws. Usage data may be anonymized.

7. Cookies & Tracking

We use cookies for session management, preferences, and analytics. You may control cookies via browser.

8. International Data Transfers

Data may be transferred outside India with appropriate safeguards.

9. Your Rights

You may request access, correction, deletion, or restriction of your personal data. Contact: info@visble.ai

10. Security

We use industry-standard safeguards but no system is 100% secure. Protect your credentials.

11. Children’s Privacy

Our Services are not for users under 13. We do not knowingly collect from minors.

12. Policy Updates

We may update this Policy. Continued use means acceptance of updates.

13. Contact Information

United Innovation Hub (Visble)
Wework, Two Horizon Centre, Gurugram, Haryana – 122009
Email: info@visble.ai

item.title: Generative Engine Optimization Playbook- Your SEO Strategies Need a Fast Upgrade
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Generative Engine Optimization Playbook- Your SEO Strategies Need a Fast Upgrade

Isha Sachdeva

Founder, visble.ai

Ranking on Google is no longer just about keywords; it’s about keeping up with AI.

If your SEO strategy still revolves around meta tags and backlinks alone, it’s time for a reality check. You can't play by the old-school tactics anymore since Generative Search Experience is rapidly gaining momentum.

ChatGPT reached 365 billion annual searches in just two years, 5.5 times faster than Google, which took 11 years to hit the same milestone. This shows that users are now shifting to AI for answers faster than ever.

What's even more surprising is that more than 61% of Gen Z users regularly utilise generative AI tools for search. They trust AI-generated answers, and they convert better too.

Do you know that ChatGPT's conversion rate is far better than Google or Bing? It delivered the highest lead-to-close rate across all search platforms.

Meanwhile, Google still owns the top of the funnel with 58% traffic share, but its downstream efficiency is slipping. ChatGPT turns clicks into customers more effectively, proving this isn’t just a Gen Z trend but a full-funnel shift.

It is a revolution, not just progress!

Keep reading our Generative Engine Optimization Playbook, where we show you how to stay visible as well as ahead in an AI-first search world!

Key Takeaways

  • Think of GEO & SEO as a power duo. The one ranks you, the other gets you cited in AI answers.
  • AI loves clear & structured content that effectively solves real user questions.
  • Find out whether AI is mentioning you by monitoring your brand’s visibility & source mentions, not just keyword rankings.
  • When your brand appears in AI-generated answers, it reaches users who are highly engaged & likely to click or convert.

How Do Generative Engines Work?

It’s time to break down the inside secrets of how generative AI models work!

Generative AI engines learn from a vast amount of information available on the internet. So when you ask them something, it doesn’t just give a list of links; it understands your question to provide a clear, helpful answer, almost like a real person talking to you.

Let me walk you through the process.

Step 1- Prompt Initiation & Query Processing

It begins when a user types in a question or something they are looking for. It could be a simple query or something more complex, such as a product comparison or a how-to request.

Step 2- Intent Parsing & Content Recognition

The engine takes a moment to understand what the user wants.

  • Are they asking for product recommendations?
  • Do they want a feature comparison?
  • Are they just exploring options or ready to buy?

This part is key! It’s interpreting intent & context, which helps the AI understand not just what is being asked, but why.

Step 3- Content Retrieval & Knowledge Aggregation

Once it knows what you're looking for, the engine searches across vast amounts of data, such as websites, product pages, PDFs, and more.

It’s not just limited to traditional search results; it can pull from:

  • Real-time sources (depending on the platform)
  • Structured content (like tables or schema markup)
  • High-authority sources, it has learned to trust

It does all this in seconds, narrowing down the most relevant, credible information.

Step 4- Response Generation

Once it has gathered the most valuable pieces of information, it’s time to create a response.

Using powerful large language models (LLMs), like GPT-4 or Gemini, the engine generates a human-like answer, summarizing everything it found in a way that sounds clear & helpful.

The result is a neatly packaged answer that saves the user from having to click through 10 different links.

Platform

Search Engine/Data Source Used

 Best For

Real-Time Web Access

ChatGPT (Pro)

Bing (for browsing), internal training data

Conversational queries, shopping, coding & creative tasks

Yes

Gemini (Google)

Google Search + LLM capabilities

Smart summaries, shopping, research & fact-finding

Yes

Microsoft Copilot

GPT-4 + Bing + Microsoft integrations

Emails, spreadsheets & Microsoft 365 productivity

Yes

Perplexity AI

Own Search Index + its own AI stack

Research with citations and web-based results

Yes

Claude (Anthropic)

Brave, internal training data

Long-form writing, analysis & safer conversations

Yes

What is Generative Engine Optimization?

Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) is the process of influencing AI search engines, like ChatGPT or Gemini, to improve visibility and website traffic in AI searches.

Instead of just using keywords, GEO focuses on creating authoritative content, increasing the frequency of your brand’s share of voice, and earning trust through consistent, credible information.

AI-powered search engines do more than just index or rank websites. It provides a summarized & personalized response to the answer using a wide range of sources (websites) from the internet.

Whereas traditional search engines would provide results for a user's query as a list of “blue links” organized by relevance & optimization, AI models can provide detailed answers from a variety of sources & contexts.

SEO vs. GEO- What’s the Real Difference?

SEO & GEO both aim to improve your brand’s visibility online, but they work in different ways.

SEO targets ranking on traditional search engines, such as Google.

But GEO aims to get your content featured in AI-generated responses on platforms like ChatGPT, Gemini, or Perplexity.

Here are some of the most notable differences!

Objective

SEO

The goal is to rank higher on traditional search engines, such as Google or Bing. The higher you rank, the more clicks & traffic you receive.

For example, optimising a blog so it ranks in the top 3 results on Google for keywords like affordable project management software.

GEO

The goal is to appear as a cited source or recommendation in responses generated by AI models. Especially for commercial prompts like best CRM tools for startups, being one of the most recommended brands is a new visibility win.

For example, appearing as a recommendation on a prompt like- “What’s the best budget-friendly project management software for startups” for the SaaS/project management company.

Content Focus

SEO

It focuses on long-form, keyword-optimized content. The idea is to match search intent & signal relevance to Google’s algorithm.

For example, a 2,000-word blog packed with high-volume keywords, headings, & internal links to rank for multiple queries.

GEO

It focuses on expert-driven, structured content. AI models require easily digestible & trustworthy content to cite. The FAQs, tables, real world case studies & examples, and concise summaries are most effective.

For example, a 1,000-word guide with bullet points, FAQs, schemas & citations. This makes it easy for ChatGPT to pick up insights as well as references for your content.

The key difference here is that SEO content often draws the user in to explore more. However, GEO content aims to answer questions directly or get cited without requiring a click.

User Interaction

SEO

Users typically click a link & visit your site to explore your content.

For example, a user Googles their query to scroll through the results. Then, click your blog post for the complete guide.

GEO

Users get direct answers inside the AI platform. They may visit your site as well as your brand is seen & trusted as a cited source.

For example, a user asks ChatGPT, Which CRM is best for small businesses. Instead of showing a list of links, ChatGPT generates an answer, and your website is cited right within that response as a trusted source.

The best part is that the user may not even need to click through to your site to trust your brand. You’re already part of the conversation.

Primary Metrics

SEO

  • Keyword ranking
  • Number of backlinks
  • Domain authority

Search Engine Optimization is all about making your website rank higher on search engines like Google, so people can find you when they’re actively searching for something you offer.

GEO

  • Brand visibility
  • Source mentions
  • Brand mentions
  • Sentiment score

It is focused on how often AI engines reference your content, as well as the number of people who visit or engage with your site.

For example, ChatGPT recommends your brand in response to 80 out of 100 queries related to your domain.

Track GEO Metrics Now

Optimization Style

SEO

Focuses on on-page elements, such as meta tags & header tags, as well as backlink building.

For example, you optimize a web page by adding keywords & building backlinks.

GEO

Focuses on structured data (schemas), clear content formatting & credibility.

For example, you can create a blog with a clear structure & apply schema markup, making it easier for AI to cite.

GEO vs. SEO Key Difference

Aspect

GEO

SEO

Objective

Rank high on Google/Bing

Cite in AI-generated answers

Content Type

Keyword-optimized, long-form

Structured, expert-driven, easy to summarise

User Interaction

Click to read full content

Get quick answers; brand cited directly

Metrics Tracked

Traffic, DA, backlinks

Citation count, brand mentions, sentiment score

Optimization Style

Meta tags, backlinks

Schema markup, FAQs, tables, examples

Correlation between GEO and SEO- Where do they meet?

Adopting an SEO-centric approach alone is no longer sufficient. Generative AI engines operate under a different set of rules, so although SEO still counts, GEO requires efforts beyond SEO.

Let’s explore where the two overlap as well as why you need both.

Content Visibility

Both SEO & GEO aim for visibility, whether it's ranking on the first page of Google or appearing in a ChatGPT response. They’re designed to help your brand stand out organically on search engines (AI/Traditional)

Content Quality & User Experience

The great content wins remains the same. GEO & SEO both prioritise high-quality, relevant, as well as trustworthy content that

  • Answers user questions
  • Demonstrates expertise
  • Follow E-E-A-T guidelines

The better your content, the more likely it is to rank well as well as be cited by AI engines.

In addition, providing the right information to the user matters for both GEO and SEO. Your content needs to be

  • Easy to read
  • Well-structured
  • Helpful & clear

Good UX improves performance for all users, whether they are humans reading your blog or AI systems scanning it for answers.

Data Insights & Analytics

Both GEO and SEO rely on data, tracking metrics like

  • What content is being seen
  • How well the content is performing
  • How much is the traffic and conversion

Your strategy can be fine-tuned with the help of this feedback loop.

Technical Optimization Still Counts

Quick page loads, mobile-friendliness, structured data, as well as crawlability are all essential for search engine optimization as well as for GEO.

At the end, you are supposed to be indexed on the search engine to be cited on AI search platforms.

Google & ChatGPT might disregard your page if it is unorganized.

Building Authority

SEO has long relied on backlinks & domain authority. GEO examines content credibility & trustworthiness and your brand mention in authoritative sources. Your authority increases your visibility in both cases, so the more credible you are, the more likely you are to be ranked or cited.

Ongoing Adaptation

Just like SEO evolves with Google’s algorithm updates, GEO changes as AI models improve. What worked last year may not work tomorrow. Continuous learning is essential for staying visible & relevant in both spaces.

Must-Track Metrics for AI Search Optimization

Generative Engine Optimization is uncharted ground for most marketers. But just like SEO, you can’t optimize without some key metrics. The most successful brands are already tracking specific metrics that help them appear more frequently in AI-generated responses.

Here’s a breakdown of the most essential GEO metrics you should be tracking regularly.

Get a Free AEO Report

Brand Visibility

Brand Visibility indicates how frequently your brand appears in prompts related to your niche or industry across AI search engines. It provides an overview of whether LLMs see you as a credible brand when answering relevant user questions. It is a highly valuable metric, especially for commercial prompt analysis.

For example, if you're trying to win prompts like Best CRM for startups or Affordable project management tools, you want your brand to show up naturally in the AI’s response. This metric tells you if you’re showing up.

(Snippet)

Source Mention

This is your AI citation score, which is one of the most direct indicators of your success in GEO.

Let’s say an AI model references 15,000 unique sources while responding to 1,000 prompts in your industry. You can see how many more citations feature your website compared to your competition's using the Source Mention metric.

Why does it matter? Mentions of your source in AI responses bring in traffic & credibility without the need for a click, in contrast to traditional SEO.

More mentions = More brand exposure = More high-intent users entering your funnel

Brand Mention

You can see how frequently your brand name is mentioned in the other source citations, across both commercial & informational prompts.

Why does it matter? The more your brand name appears in citations across other sources, even without a direct link, the more it signals to AI models that your brand is trusted & frequently discussed. This increases your chances of being recommended in AI-generated responses as well as cited directly in future answers.

It’s not just about visibility, it’s about brand positioning. The more you're mentioned, the more you're perceived as an authority in your industry. Over time, this helps boost your influence in both commercial & informational prompts.

Average Ranking (Within AI Responses)

Ranking at the top is essential in traditional SEO. The goal of GEO is to be ranked or cited early in the AI-generated answer.

It carries more weight if your brand is mentioned first or early in a list or comparison. Similar to how a highly ranked Google result influences conversion rates, this subtle positioning can have a similar effect.

Top Sources

Here you can see which websites are most often used by the AI when answering questions related to your industry.

Knowing which sources AI relies on the most allows you to outperform them by analyzing the kind of content, structure, as well as authority they use. This insight will show you who is already a top source, along with what they're doing correctly, if you aren't one yet.

By identifying news, blogs, review platforms, and marketplaces, you can strategically aim to get your brand mentioned on the top performing sources. It boosts your credibility with LLMs, making it more likely you’ll get cited in future AI-generated answers.

Top Source Categories

This metric indicates the types of websites AI models trust most when answering queries in your domain, including marketplaces, review sites, blogs, wikis, or news outlets.

Why is this important? Because it helps you understand where AI is pulling its answers from, so you can focus on the content distribution in the same categories..

Let’s make it clearer with an example. 

For commercial queries, AI often draws from marketplaces, product review platforms, & comparison blogs. These platforms influence purchasing decisions, so getting mentioned here increases your chances of being cited in high-intent prompts.

For informational queries, AI tends to cite Wikipedia, educational blogs, or explainer sites that are seen as authoritative & neutral.

This metric helps you understand where the AI is focusing, providing a roadmap to get there.

Trending Conversations

This metric tracks what AI models are repeatedly bringing up in their responses across various prompts in your industry.

These recurring themes help you spot what’s gaining traction. By aligning your content with them early, you boost your chances of being cited in future AI-generated responses.

Being early to rank or be cited in a trending question gives you a head start in visibility.

The Important Aspects of Generative Engine Optimization

GEO Element

Why It Matters for GEO

Content Relevance

The more relevant your content, the more likely AI is to use it in responses.

Content Quality & Depth

AI prioritises in-depth, well-explained content that adds value.

Trustworthiness & Credibility

High credibility means higher chances of being cited by AI for factual queries.

AI Crawlability & Structured Data

Structured content is easier for AI to process & pull into responses.

Topical Authority & Expertise

AI favours authoritative voices in any niche. It builds trust in your content over time.

Content Freshness & Timeliness

AI models prioritise up-to-date content, particularly in tech or trending topics.

Citations & Mentions

Getting cited means your content is being trusted. It increases in visibility.

Data Frequency & Consistency

Regular output signals are relevant & active, both of which are appreciated by AI models.

Verifiable Performance Metrics

AI prefers data-backed content it can trust & quote.

Technical Performance

A poorly performing site can impact your crawlability & AI visibility.

Localisation

Local relevance enables AI to provide the most relevant answer to location-based or geo-specific queries.

AI Sentiment

AI picks up tone. The content that feels biased or unclear might be deprioritised.

Search Engine Indexing

Pages that are effectively indexed by related search engines are more likely to be included in the data used by AI models.

Social Proof, Reviews & UGC

AI uses this as evidence of credibility & trust, especially in product or service-related prompts.

Social Signals

It’s a secondary signal, but AI may consider viral or well-discussed content more impactful.

Step-by-Step Guide on Generative Engine Optimization

Before we get into how to optimize for AI engines, let’s take a second to talk about the why.

What’s the real goal of Generative Engine Optimization? It’s about making sure your brand shows up as a credible recommendation or citation in AI-generated answers, whether someone’s using ChatGPT, Gemini, or any other AI search tool.

The two primary goals are:

  • Improving Brand Visibility- You want your brand to show up in the response as a top recommendation. It's also great for commercial & informational prompts, where authority & trust are built.

Because these placements in AI responses are driving high-intent traffic, these users are already deep into the decision-making process.

  • Increasing Source Mentions- The more often your website is cited in AI-generated responses, whether it’s a blog or product page, the better.

Build Data-Backed AI Search Optimization Startegy

  • Visibility Analysis
  • Content Analysis
  • Citation Analysis
  • Competitor's Analysis
Sign up for free Learn more

It builds credibility & authority in the industry, where users are actively looking for answers. With more qualified visitors to your site, you ultimately achieve more conversions.

Let’s dive right into the steps: 

Step 1- Analyse the Influencing Factors

You need a good understanding of the landscape before you try to compete in AI-powered search. Here’s how to get started:

Specify Your Industry & the Right Topics

Begin with broad terms before narrowing down to the most important topics or questions. While researching your industry, consider the kinds of questions that consumers would ask an AI assistant, starting at the top of the funnel & moving down the funnel.

Spot the Key Players

To find out which brands or publications show up most often in AI responses, use visble.ai Generative Engine Optimization tool to perform an analysis on hundreds of searches. Unexpected sources, such as specialised blogs or industry newsletters, can end up with more AI citations than well-known media sites.

Study the Winning Content Formats

Learn which kinds of content is most often mentioned by different AI models, whether it is in-depth how-to manuals, product comparison tables, or expert Q&As. You can direct your content strategy by learning the successful types.

Keep Tabs on Your Results to Find Out Trends

Instead of manually logging your results in a spreadsheet, use Visible.ai to simplify the entire process.

With Visible.ai, you can:

  • Run large scale prompt experiments across different AI models (like ChatGPT, Gemini, and more)
  • Create & manage multiple campaigns
  • Track which sources, brands, as well as content formats are being cited
  • Monitor trends & performance over time

Once you’ve tracked a solid batch of prompts, look for patterns. This helps you identify which type of content consistently appear, as well as some unexpected brands that dominate specific topics.

By the time you finish this step, you will have a good idea of who’s dominating AI responses or what kinds of content is performing well. You identify where your brand has an opportunity to make a difference. This research becomes the foundation of your entire GEO strategy.

This research becomes the foundation of your entire GEO strategy!

Step 2- Make Your Content AI Search-Friendly

Now that you’ve done your homework in Step 1, it's time to start building the kind of content that gets picked up by AI-powered search tools. Think of this step as creating AI-ready content that is structured & super easy for AI models to cite in their responses.

AI Loves Structure, Pay Attention to Clarity & Structure

AI models prefer organized, easy-to-digest information. So give them:

  • Clear headings & subheadings
  • Bullet points & numbered lists
  • FAQ sections & step-by-step guides
  • Tables like Pros & Cons, Feature Comparisons, or Pricing Breakdowns
  • Semantic headings & structure

The more structured it is, the easier it is for AI to reference. Hence, it is more likely that your brand will show up in its answers.

Create Comparison-Ready Content

Help the AI by creating side-by-side comparisons or detailed breakdowns that answer user questions.

Add Unique Insights & Expert Input

Don’t just repeat what everyone else is saying. Add something unique to your content.

  • Pull in quotes or perspectives from your internal experts.
  • Share proprietary data or original research.
  • Include real customer examples, tips, or case studies.
  • Explain what your brand does differently & why it matters.

This gives AI models something original & authoritative to help you stand out where everyone else is the same.

Speak the AI’s Language (Plain & Precise)

In fact, the clearer your language, the better.

  • Avoid buzzwords & vague phrases.
  • Use specific terms, especially for products, services, or features.
  • Consider how a user would phrase a question, and try to answer it in their language.

By the end of this step, you should have a library of structured as well as citation-friendly content. This type of content is easily understood & referenced by AI in its answers.

Step 3- Smart Distribution for Maximum AI Visibility

You’ve created structured, high-quality content that’s built for AI. Now what?

It’s time to distribute your content across trusted citations that generative engines pull from.

The Secret Sources Behind AI Citations

Here’s a secret: not all citations come from big-name media outlets. AI models often reference niche sites, Reddit threads, or industry-specific forums. Now, reach out to them. Pitch a guest article or fresh data that adds value. 

The goal is to get your voice on their platform, as AI already views them as trustworthy.

Use PR to Earn AI Trust

Good old PR still works. If you land features or quotes in high-authority publications, that content becomes part of the training data or reference set for AI models. Sponsored content or strategic partnerships on high-traffic industry blogs can give you a strong edge.

Don’t Forget Your Channels

While third-party credibility is powerful, don’t ignore your website. It’s your direct line to AI engines. 

Make sure your blog or knowledge base:

  • It is well-structured & easy to crawl.
  • Uses schema markup where possible.
  • Gets updated regularly with relevant content.
  • Answers real-world prompts users might ask.

Sometimes, AI will reference your content directly if it trusts your site enough.

By the end of this step, you’ll have a distribution plan that helps place your AI-optimized content in sources already favoured by AI models. The more often you appear in trusted outlets, the more likely you are to get cited in generative responses.

Step 4- Technical GEO- Make Your Site AI-Ready

Now it’s time to make sure the technical foundation of your website supports all that great work. Think of this step as setting up the backstage so that AI engines can easily find, understand, &  trust your content.

Make Your Site Easy to Crawl & Index

AI engines rely on web crawlers to discover content. If your site is hard to navigate or slow to load, AI models might miss or avoid your pages. Here’s what to focus on:

  • Fast loading speeds because nobody likes to wait, especially AI crawlers scanning tons of pages.
  • Use logical menus, internal links, as well as clear URL structures to prevent bots from getting lost.
  • AI engines prioritize mobile usability since most users search on their phones.

Use Schema Markup to Speak AI’s Language

Schema markup is like adding metadata tags to your pages that help AI understand exactly what your content is about. It instructs the AI engine to determine whether a page is a product, FAQ, or something else. This extra clarity boosts your chances of being cited accurately.

  • Implement structured data using JSON-LD (the recommended format).
  • Prioritize schema for your most important pages, like product pages, blog posts, or FAQs.

Implement an LLMS.txt File for Better AI Visibility

One of the newer, powerful tools to guide AI models is the LLMS.txt file. Think of it as a roadmap specifically designed for language models & generative AI to better understand your website. 

Here are some best practices to create an effective LLMS.txt!

Keep it simple

Use clear, concise language with a clean Markdown structure so AI can easily parse it.

Provide a summary

Begin with a brief description of your website’s purpose to provide context.

List key sections

Highlight important areas, such as Services, Blog, & Documentation, with brief explanations.

Use descriptive links

Link to main pages only, not every single post, as well as describe why those pages matter.

Ensure AI readability

Use plain text formatting, avoid fancy styling, and use straight quotes (‘ or ’) instead of curly ones to prevent encoding issues.

Update regularly

As your site evolves, keep your LLMS.txt current to maintain AI accuracy & relevance.

Place it in the root directory

This makes it easily discoverable by AI engines scanning your site.

Adding an LLMS.txt file provides AI models with a clearer understanding of your site’s structure & content priorities, making it easier for them to surface your brand in relevant generative responses.

Ensure Your Site is Secure & Trustworthy

Security matters, not just for your users but also for AI engines. Having HTTPS (SSL certificates) signals that your site is safe, boosting trust & crawl priority.

By the end of Step 4, your website will be technically optimized for AI discovery & citation, leading the way for stronger visibility as well as authority in generative search results.

Step 5- Measure the outcome

Now comes the essential part- tracking your impact! It is vital to measure the performance of your content in AI responses. Let’s see how to do that.

Start with the Right Tools

Make use of AI visibility tracking tools to help you monitor how often, where, or in what context your brand shows up in AI-generated answers.

Here are a few metrics you’ll want to keep track of.

  • Visibility Score- Think of this as your overall AI presence rating.
  • Brand Mentions- Track how many times your brand or products are getting mentioned by AI.
  • Citation Sources- See who is referencing you & why.

Track Before & After Changes

Every time you publish a new piece of content, especially a well-structured guide or comparison, monitor the effect.

Spot the Gaps

If you are not mentioned in the answers to specific prompts, it shows you where to focus next. Search those prompts to analyze & turn missed opportunities into content ideas that align directly with user intent.

By the end of this step, you’ll have a live dashboard or simple report that gives you a clear view of your performance in AI answers. These insights let you make strategic moves instead of playing guessing games.

Step 6- Iterate for better performance

GEO isn’t a set-it-and-forget-it game!

The AI world is constantly evolving, and the brands that succeed are those that adapt. Here is how to do that.

Scale What’s Working, Repeat Your Wins

If something’s clicking, run with it. Figure out what’s gaining traction, then scale those wins.

Let Go of What’s Not

Not every content format will hit. If your long-form pieces never get cited, consider creating something more structured, such as a how-to guide or a side-by-side comparison. The key is learning, not clinging.

Keep Tabs on the Bigger Picture

New AI models & answer engines are popping up all the time. If you're only optimizing for one model, you could miss out on growing ecosystems that your audience is already using.
Every few weeks, revisit the landscape & make adjustments as needed.

Treat It Like a Living Strategy

What works today might not work six months from now. AI models are updated, or new content types emerge. So, try monthly check-ins or ongoing content audits to help you stay proactive, not reactive.

Over time, you’ll develop a repeatable feedback loop. It becomes a system that constantly improves your brand’s visibility in AI responses.

Think of it as your brand’s real-time reputation engine for AI search.

The Final Thoughts

Generative Engine Optimization is the future of how people discover brands or products.  If your strategy is still built around old-school SEO alone, you’re missing where the real action is happening- inside AI-powered answers.

The rules have changed, but the opportunity has never been greater. It's time to upgrade your SEO playbook to meet your audience where they’re searching, due to AI.

It\u2019s time to break down the inside secrets of how generative AI models work!<\/span><\/p>

Generative AI engines<\/span><\/a> learn from a vast amount of information available on the internet. So when you ask them something, it doesn\u2019t just give a list of links; it understands your question to provide a clear, helpful answer, almost like a real person talking to you.<\/span><\/p>

Let me walk you through the process.<\/span><\/p>

Step 1- Prompt Initiation & Query Processing<\/h3>

It begins when a user types in a question or something they are looking for. It could be a simple query or something more complex, such as a product comparison or a how-to request.<\/span><\/p>

Step 2- Intent Parsing & Content Recognition<\/h3>

The engine takes a moment to understand what the user wants.<\/span><\/p>

  • Are they asking for product recommendations?<\/span><\/li>
  • Do they want a feature comparison?<\/span><\/li>
  • Are they just exploring options or ready to buy?<\/span><\/li><\/ul>

    This part is key! It\u2019s interpreting intent & context, which helps the AI understand not just what is being asked, but why.<\/span><\/p>

    Step 3- Content Retrieval & Knowledge Aggregation<\/h3>

    Once it knows what you're looking for, the engine searches across vast amounts of data, such as websites, product pages, PDFs, and more.<\/span><\/p>

    It\u2019s not just limited to traditional search results; it can pull from:<\/span><\/p>

    • Real-time sources (depending on the platform)<\/span><\/li>
    • Structured content (like tables or schema markup)<\/span><\/li>
    • High-authority sources, it has learned to trust<\/span><\/li><\/ul>

      It does all this in seconds, narrowing down the most relevant, credible information.<\/span><\/p>

      Step 4- Response Generation<\/h3>

      Once it has gathered the most valuable pieces of information, it\u2019s time to create a response.<\/span><\/p>

      Using powerful<\/span> large language models (LLMs)<\/span><\/a>, like GPT-4 or Gemini, the engine generates a human-like answer, summarizing everything it found in a way that sounds clear & helpful.<\/span><\/p>

      The result is a neatly packaged answer that saves the user from having to click through 10 different links.<\/span><\/p>

      Platform<\/strong><\/p><\/td>

      Search Engine\/Data Source Used<\/strong><\/p><\/td>

      \u00a0Best For<\/strong><\/p><\/td>

      Real-Time Web Access<\/strong><\/p><\/td><\/tr>

      ChatGPT (Pro)<\/strong><\/p><\/td>

      Bing (for browsing), internal training data<\/p><\/td>

      Conversational queries, shopping, coding & creative tasks<\/p><\/td>

      Yes<\/p><\/td><\/tr>

      Gemini (Google)<\/strong><\/p><\/td>

      Google Search + LLM capabilities<\/p><\/td>

      Smart summaries, shopping, research & fact-finding<\/p><\/td>

      Yes<\/p><\/td><\/tr>

      Microsoft Copilot<\/strong><\/p><\/td>

      GPT-4 + Bing + Microsoft integrations<\/p><\/td>

      Emails, spreadsheets & Microsoft 365 productivity<\/p><\/td>

      Yes<\/p><\/td><\/tr>

      Perplexity AI<\/strong><\/p><\/td>

      Own Search Index + its own AI stack<\/p><\/td>

      Research with citations and web-based results<\/p><\/td>

      Yes<\/p><\/td><\/tr>

      Claude (Anthropic)<\/strong><\/p><\/td>

      Brave, internal training data<\/p><\/td>

      Long-form writing, analysis & safer conversations<\/p><\/td>

      Yes<\/p><\/td><\/tr><\/tbody><\/table>","__globals__":{"link_color":"globals\/colors?id=primary","link_hover_color":"globals\/colors?id=accent"},"typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Manrope","typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"px","size":35,"sizes":[]},"_padding_mobile":{"unit":"px","top":"0","right":"20","bottom":"0","left":"20","isLinked":false}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"}],"isInner":true},{"id":"74a5e67","elType":"container","settings":{"content_width":"full","margin":{"unit":"px","top":"0","right":"0","bottom":"15","left":"0","isLinked":false},"_element_id":"generativeengine","padding_mobile":{"unit":"px","top":"0","right":"20","bottom":"0","left":"20","isLinked":false}},"elements":[{"id":"cb9ff8f","elType":"widget","settings":{"title":"What is Generative Engine Optimization?","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Plus Jakarta Sans","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":50,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_tablet":{"unit":"px","size":40,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile_extra":{"unit":"px","size":36,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile":{"unit":"px","size":26,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_weight":"600","typography_line_height":{"unit":"em","size":1.2,"sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_laptop":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_tablet_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_mobile_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"text_stroke_text_stroke_type":"yes","_margin":{"unit":"px","top":"20","right":"0","bottom":"20","left":"0","isLinked":false},"_element_width":"inherit","_css_classes":"txt-gradient"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"95f0b45","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

      Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) is the process of influencing AI search engines, like ChatGPT or Gemini, to improve visibility and website traffic in AI searches.<\/i><\/b><\/p>

      Instead of just using keywords, GEO focuses on creating authoritative content, increasing the frequency of your brand\u2019s share of voice, and earning trust through consistent, credible information.<\/span><\/p>

      AI-powered search engines do more than just index or rank websites. It provides a summarized & personalized response to the answer using a wide range of sources (websites) from the internet.<\/span><\/p>

      Whereas traditional search engines would provide results for a user's query as a list of \u201cblue links\u201d organized by relevance & optimization, AI models can provide detailed answers from a variety of sources & contexts.<\/span><\/p>","__globals__":{"link_color":"globals\/colors?id=primary","link_hover_color":"globals\/colors?id=accent"},"typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Manrope","typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"px","size":35,"sizes":[]}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"}],"isInner":true},{"id":"28cbf78","elType":"container","settings":{"content_width":"full","margin":{"unit":"px","top":"0","right":"0","bottom":"0","left":"0","isLinked":false},"_element_id":"seovsgeo","padding_mobile":{"unit":"px","top":"0","right":"20","bottom":"0","left":"20","isLinked":false}},"elements":[{"id":"8670f3a","elType":"widget","settings":{"title":"SEO vs. GEO- What\u2019s the Real Difference?","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Plus Jakarta Sans","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":50,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_tablet":{"unit":"px","size":40,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile_extra":{"unit":"px","size":36,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile":{"unit":"px","size":26,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_weight":"600","typography_line_height":{"unit":"em","size":1.2,"sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_laptop":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_tablet_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_mobile_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"text_stroke_text_stroke_type":"yes","_margin":{"unit":"px","top":"20","right":"0","bottom":"20","left":"0","isLinked":false},"_element_width":"inherit","_css_classes":"txt-gradient"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"e61c429","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

      SEO & GEO both aim to improve your brand\u2019s visibility online, but they work in different ways.<\/span><\/p>

      SEO targets ranking on traditional search engines, such as Google.<\/span><\/p>

      But GEO aims to get your content featured in AI-generated responses on platforms like ChatGPT, Gemini, or Perplexity.<\/span><\/p>

      Here are some of the most notable differences!<\/span><\/p>

      Objective<\/h3>

      SEO<\/strong><\/p>

      The goal is to rank higher on traditional search engines, such as Google or Bing. The higher you rank, the more clicks & traffic you receive.<\/span><\/p>

      For example, optimising a blog so it ranks in the top 3 results on Google for keywords like affordable project management software.<\/span><\/i><\/p>

      GEO<\/strong><\/p>

      The goal is to appear as a cited source or recommendation in responses generated by AI models. Especially for commercial prompts like best CRM tools for startups, being one of the most recommended brands is a new visibility win.<\/span><\/p>

      For example, appearing as a recommendation on a prompt like- \u201cWhat\u2019s the best budget-friendly project management software for startups\u201d for the SaaS\/project management company.<\/span><\/i><\/p>

      Content Focus<\/h3>

      SEO<\/strong><\/p>

      It focuses on long-form, keyword-optimized content. The idea is to match search intent & signal relevance to Google\u2019s algorithm.<\/span><\/p>

      For example, a 2,000-word blog packed with high-volume keywords, headings, & internal links to rank for multiple queries.<\/span><\/i><\/p>

      GEO<\/b><\/p>

      It focuses on expert-driven, structured content. AI models require easily digestible & trustworthy content to cite. The FAQs, tables, real world case studies & examples, and concise summaries are most effective.<\/span><\/p>

      For example, a 1,000-word guide with bullet points, FAQs, schemas & citations. This makes it easy for ChatGPT to pick up insights as well as references for your content.<\/span><\/i><\/p>","__globals__":{"link_color":"globals\/colors?id=primary","link_hover_color":"globals\/colors?id=accent"},"typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Manrope","typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"px","size":35,"sizes":[]}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"},{"id":"0ec9615","elType":"container","settings":{"boxed_width":{"unit":"px","size":1180,"sizes":[]},"flex_direction":"row","flex_align_items":"stretch","flex_wrap":"wrap","background_background":"classic","background_color":"#130F23","background_image":{"url":"","id":"","size":"","alt":"","source":"library"},"background_position":"center right","background_repeat":"no-repeat","border_radius":{"unit":"px","top":"16","right":"16","bottom":"16","left":"16","isLinked":true},"padding":{"unit":"px","top":"30","right":"30","bottom":"30","left":"30","isLinked":false},"padding_tablet":{"unit":"px","top":"30","right":"30","bottom":"30","left":"30","isLinked":true},"padding_mobile":{"unit":"px","top":"20","right":"20","bottom":"20","left":"20","isLinked":true},"content_width":"full"},"elements":[{"id":"8229d36","elType":"widget","settings":{"selected_icon":{"value":"fas fa-rocket","library":"fa-solid"},"title_text":"","description_text":"The key difference here is that SEO content often draws the user in to explore more. However, GEO content aims to answer questions directly or get cited without requiring a click.","position":"left","icon_space":{"unit":"px","size":24,"sizes":[]},"description_typography_typography":"custom","description_typography_font_family":"Manrope","description_typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":16,"sizes":[]},"description_typography_font_weight":"400"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"icon-box"}],"isInner":true},{"id":"127fdbe","elType":"container","settings":{"content_width":"full","margin":{"unit":"px","top":"20","right":"0","bottom":"15","left":"0","isLinked":false}},"elements":[{"id":"92c80d5","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

      User Interaction<\/strong><\/h3>

      SEO<\/b><\/p>

      Users typically click a link & visit your site to explore your content.<\/span><\/p>

      For example, a user Googles their query to scroll through the results. Then, click your blog post for the complete guide.<\/span><\/i><\/p>

      GEO<\/b><\/p>

      Users get direct answers inside the AI platform. They may visit your site as well as your brand is seen & trusted as a cited source.<\/span><\/p>

      For example, a user asks ChatGPT, Which CRM is best for small businesses. Instead of showing a list of links, ChatGPT generates an answer, and your website is cited right within that response as a trusted source.<\/span><\/i><\/p>

      The best part is that the user may not even need to click through to your site to trust your brand. You\u2019re already part of the conversation.<\/span><\/p>

      Primary Metrics<\/strong><\/h3>

      SEO<\/b><\/p>

      • Keyword ranking<\/span><\/li>
      • Number of backlinks<\/span><\/li>
      • Domain authority<\/span><\/li><\/ul>

        Search Engine Optimization is all about making your website rank higher on search engines like Google, so people can find you when they\u2019re actively searching for something you offer.<\/span><\/p>

        GEO<\/b><\/p>

        • Brand visibility<\/span><\/li>
        • Source mentions<\/span><\/li>
        • Brand mentions<\/span><\/li>
        • Sentiment score<\/span><\/li><\/ul>

          It is focused on how often AI engines reference your content, as well as the number of people who visit or engage with your site.<\/span><\/p>

          For example, ChatGPT recommends your brand in response to 80 out of 100 queries related to your domain.
          <\/span><\/i><\/p>","__globals__":{"link_color":"globals\/colors?id=primary","link_hover_color":"globals\/colors?id=accent"},"typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Manrope","typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"px","size":35,"sizes":[]}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"},{"id":"2899427","elType":"widget","settings":{"text":"Track GEO Metrics Now","align":"left","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"DM Sans","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":18,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_weight":"500","hover_color":"#000000","border_border":"solid","border_width":{"unit":"px","top":"1","right":"1","bottom":"1","left":"1","isLinked":true},"border_radius":{"unit":"px","top":"6","right":"6","bottom":"6","left":"6","isLinked":true},"text_padding":{"unit":"px","top":"12","right":"15","bottom":"12","left":"15","isLinked":false},"_element_width":"auto","_css_classes":"btn-border contactpopup","__globals__":{"background_color":"","border_color":"globals\/colors?id=accent","button_text_color":"globals\/colors?id=secondary","button_hover_border_color":"globals\/colors?id=secondary","button_background_hover_color":"globals\/colors?id=secondary"},"link":{"url":"https:\/\/app.visble.ai\/signup","is_external":"","nofollow":"","custom_attributes":""},"_margin":{"unit":"px","top":"20","right":"20","bottom":"30","left":"0","isLinked":false},"typography_font_size_mobile":{"unit":"px","size":14,"sizes":[]},"button_text_color":"#FFFFFF","background_color":"#6B52D1","button_background_hover_color":"#FFFFFF","button_hover_border_color":"#FFFFFF","border_color":"#6B52D1"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"button"},{"id":"df9766f","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

          Optimization Style<\/strong><\/h3>

          SEO<\/b><\/p>

          Focuses on on-page elements, such as meta tags & header tags, as well as backlink building.<\/span><\/p>

          For example, you optimize a web page by adding keywords & building backlinks.<\/span><\/i><\/p>

          GEO<\/b><\/p>

          Focuses on structured data (schemas), clear content formatting & credibility.<\/span><\/p>

          For example, you can create a blog with a clear structure & apply schema markup, making it easier for AI to cite.<\/span><\/i><\/p>

          GEO vs. SEO Key Difference<\/strong><\/h3>

          Aspect<\/b><\/p><\/td>

          GEO<\/b><\/p><\/td>

          SEO<\/b><\/p><\/td><\/tr>

          Objective<\/b><\/p><\/td>

          Rank high on Google\/Bing<\/span><\/p><\/td>

          Cite in AI-generated answers<\/span><\/p><\/td><\/tr>

          Content Type<\/b><\/p><\/td>

          Keyword-optimized, long-form<\/span><\/p><\/td>

          Structured, expert-driven, easy to summarise<\/span><\/p><\/td><\/tr>

          User Interaction<\/b><\/p><\/td>

          Click to read full content<\/span><\/p><\/td>

          Get quick answers; brand cited directly<\/span><\/p><\/td><\/tr>

          Metrics Tracked<\/b><\/p><\/td>

          Traffic, DA, backlinks<\/span><\/p><\/td>

          Citation count, brand mentions, sentiment score<\/span><\/p><\/td><\/tr>

          Optimization Style<\/b><\/p><\/td>

          Meta tags, backlinks<\/span><\/p><\/td>

          Schema markup, FAQs, tables, examples<\/span><\/p><\/td><\/tr><\/tbody><\/table>","__globals__":{"link_color":"globals\/colors?id=primary","link_hover_color":"globals\/colors?id=accent"},"typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Manrope","typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"px","size":35,"sizes":[]}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"},{"id":"30b206b","elType":"container","settings":{"content_width":"full","margin":{"unit":"px","top":"0","right":"0","bottom":"15","left":"0","isLinked":false},"_element_id":"correlationgeo","width_mobile":{"unit":"px","size":321.667},"_flex_size":"none","_element_width_mobile":"initial"},"elements":[{"id":"d5ab5a7","elType":"widget","settings":{"title":"Correlation between GEO and SEO- Where do they meet?","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Plus Jakarta Sans","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":50,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_tablet":{"unit":"px","size":40,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile_extra":{"unit":"px","size":36,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile":{"unit":"px","size":26,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_weight":"600","typography_line_height":{"unit":"em","size":1.2,"sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_laptop":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_tablet_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_mobile_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"text_stroke_text_stroke_type":"yes","_margin":{"unit":"px","top":"20","right":"0","bottom":"20","left":"0","isLinked":false},"_element_width":"inherit","_css_classes":"txt-gradient"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"9b0edcf","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

          Adopting an SEO-centric approach alone is no longer sufficient. Generative AI engines operate under a different set of rules, so although SEO still counts, GEO requires efforts beyond SEO.<\/span><\/p>

          Let\u2019s explore where the two overlap as well as why you need both.<\/span><\/p>

          Content Visibility<\/strong><\/h3>

          Both SEO & GEO aim for visibility, whether it's ranking on the first page of Google or appearing in a ChatGPT response. They\u2019re designed to help your brand stand out organically on search engines (AI\/Traditional)<\/span><\/p>

          Content Quality & User Experience<\/strong><\/h3>

          The great content wins remains the same. GEO & SEO both prioritise high-quality, relevant, as well as trustworthy content that<\/span><\/p>

          • Answers user questions<\/span><\/li>
          • Demonstrates expertise<\/span><\/li>
          • Follow <\/span>E-E-A-T guidelines<\/span><\/a><\/li><\/ul>

            The better your content, the more likely it is to rank well as well as be cited by AI engines.<\/span><\/p>

            In addition, providing the right information to the user matters for both GEO and SEO. Your content needs to be<\/span><\/p>

            • Easy to read<\/span><\/li>
            • Well-structured<\/span><\/li>
            • Helpful & clear<\/span><\/li><\/ul>

              Good UX improves performance for all users, whether they are humans reading your blog or AI systems scanning it for answers.<\/span><\/p>

              Data Insights & Analytics<\/strong><\/h3>

              Both GEO and SEO rely on data, tracking metrics like<\/span><\/p>

              • What content is being seen<\/span><\/li>
              • How well the content is performing<\/span><\/li>
              • How much is the traffic and conversion<\/span><\/li><\/ul>

                Your strategy can be fine-tuned with the help of this feedback loop.<\/span><\/p>

                Technical Optimization Still Counts<\/strong><\/h3>

                Quick page loads, mobile-friendliness, structured data, as well as crawlability are all essential for search engine optimization as well as for GEO.<\/span><\/p>

                At the end, you are supposed to be indexed on the search engine to be cited on AI search platforms.<\/span><\/p>

                Google & ChatGPT might disregard your page if it is unorganized.<\/span><\/p>

                Building Authority<\/strong><\/h3>

                SEO has long relied on backlinks & domain authority. GEO examines content credibility & trustworthiness and your brand mention in authoritative sources. Your authority increases your visibility in both cases, so the more credible you are, the more likely you are to be ranked or cited.<\/span><\/p>

                Ongoing Adaptation<\/strong><\/h3>

                Just like SEO evolves with Google\u2019s algorithm updates, GEO changes as AI models improve. What worked last year may not work tomorrow. Continuous learning is essential for staying visible & relevant in both spaces.<\/span><\/p>","__globals__":{"link_color":"globals\/colors?id=primary","link_hover_color":"globals\/colors?id=accent"},"typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Manrope","typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"px","size":35,"sizes":[]}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"},{"id":"4b9b7a0","elType":"container","settings":{"content_width":"full","margin":{"unit":"px","top":"0","right":"0","bottom":"15","left":"0","isLinked":false},"_element_id":"musttrackmetrics"},"elements":[{"id":"51b4e30","elType":"widget","settings":{"title":"Must-Track Metrics for AI Search Optimization","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Plus Jakarta Sans","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":50,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_tablet":{"unit":"px","size":40,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile_extra":{"unit":"px","size":36,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile":{"unit":"px","size":26,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_weight":"600","typography_line_height":{"unit":"em","size":1.2,"sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_laptop":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_tablet_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_mobile_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"text_stroke_text_stroke_type":"yes","_margin":{"unit":"px","top":"20","right":"0","bottom":"20","left":"0","isLinked":false},"_element_width":"inherit","_css_classes":"txt-gradient"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"717bfcd","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

                Generative Engine Optimization is uncharted ground for most marketers. But just like SEO, you can\u2019t optimize without some key metrics. The most successful brands are already tracking specific metrics that help them appear more frequently in AI-generated responses.<\/span><\/p>

                Here\u2019s a breakdown of the most essential GEO metrics you should be tracking regularly.<\/span><\/p>","__globals__":{"link_color":"globals\/colors?id=primary","link_hover_color":"globals\/colors?id=accent"},"typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Manrope","typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"px","size":35,"sizes":[]}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"},{"id":"8b0b42f","elType":"widget","settings":{"text":"Get a Free AEO Report","align":"left","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"DM Sans","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":18,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_weight":"500","hover_color":"#000000","border_border":"solid","border_width":{"unit":"px","top":"1","right":"1","bottom":"1","left":"1","isLinked":true},"border_radius":{"unit":"px","top":"6","right":"6","bottom":"6","left":"6","isLinked":true},"text_padding":{"unit":"px","top":"12","right":"15","bottom":"12","left":"15","isLinked":false},"_element_width":"auto","_css_classes":"btn-border contactpopup","__globals__":{"background_color":"","border_color":"globals\/colors?id=accent","button_text_color":"globals\/colors?id=secondary","button_hover_border_color":"globals\/colors?id=secondary","button_background_hover_color":"globals\/colors?id=secondary"},"link":{"url":"https:\/\/app.visble.ai\/signup","is_external":"","nofollow":"","custom_attributes":""},"_margin":{"unit":"px","top":"0","right":"20","bottom":"20","left":"0","isLinked":false},"typography_font_size_mobile":{"unit":"px","size":14,"sizes":[]},"button_text_color":"#FFFFFF","background_color":"#6B52D1","button_background_hover_color":"#FFFFFF","button_hover_border_color":"#FFFFFF","border_color":"#6B52D1"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"button"},{"id":"9bbb6ba","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

                Brand Visibility<\/strong><\/h3>

                Brand Visibility indicates how frequently your brand appears in prompts related to your niche or industry across AI search engines. It provides an overview of whether LLMs see you as a credible brand when answering relevant user questions. It is a highly valuable metric, especially for commercial prompt analysis.<\/span><\/i><\/p>

                For example, if you're trying to win prompts like Best CRM for startups or Affordable project management tools, you want your brand to show up naturally in the AI\u2019s response. This metric tells you if you\u2019re showing up.<\/span><\/i><\/p>

                (Snippet)<\/strong><\/p>

                Source Mention<\/strong><\/h3>

                This is your AI citation score, which is one of the most direct indicators of your success in GEO.<\/span><\/i><\/p>

                Let\u2019s say an AI model references 15,000 unique sources while responding to 1,000 prompts in your industry. You can see how many more citations feature your website compared to your competition's using the Source Mention metric.<\/span><\/i><\/p>

                Why does it matter? Mentions of your source in AI responses bring in traffic & credibility without the need for a click, in contrast to traditional SEO.<\/span><\/p>","__globals__":{"link_color":"globals\/colors?id=primary","link_hover_color":"globals\/colors?id=accent"},"typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Manrope","typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"px","size":35,"sizes":[]}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"},{"id":"735763c","elType":"container","settings":{"boxed_width":{"unit":"px","size":1180,"sizes":[]},"flex_direction":"row","flex_align_items":"stretch","flex_wrap":"wrap","background_background":"classic","background_color":"#130F23","background_image":{"url":"","id":"","size":"","alt":"","source":"library"},"background_position":"center
