item.title: Checkout
Refund and Returns Policy
item.content:encoded:
This is a sample page.

Overview

Our refund and returns policy lasts 30 days. If 30 days have passed since your purchase, we can’t offer you a full refund or exchange.

To be eligible for a return, your item must be unused and in the same condition that you received it. It must also be in the original packaging.

Several types of goods are exempt from being returned. Perishable goods such as food, flowers, newspapers or magazines cannot be returned. We also do not accept products that are intimate or sanitary goods, hazardous materials, or flammable liquids or gases.

Additional non-returnable items:

  • Gift cards
  • Downloadable software products
  • Some health and personal care items

To complete your return, we require a receipt or proof of purchase.

Please do not send your purchase back to the manufacturer.

There are certain situations where only partial refunds are granted:

  • Book with obvious signs of use
  • CD, DVD, VHS tape, software, video game, cassette tape, or vinyl record that has been opened.
  • Any item not in its original condition, is damaged or missing parts for reasons not due to our error.
  • Any item that is returned more than 30 days after delivery

Refunds

Once your return is received and inspected, we will send you an email to notify you that we have received your returned item. We will also notify you of the approval or rejection of your refund.

If you are approved, then your refund will be processed, and a credit will automatically be applied to your credit card or original method of payment, within a certain amount of days.

Late or missing refunds

If you haven’t received a refund yet, first check your bank account again.

Then contact your credit card company, it may take some time before your refund is officially posted.

Next contact your bank. There is often some processing time before a refund is posted.

If you’ve done all of this and you still have not received your refund yet, please contact us at {email address}.

Sale items

Only regular priced items may be refunded. Sale items cannot be refunded.

Exchanges

We only replace items if they are defective or damaged. If you need to exchange it for the same item, send us an email at {email address} and send your item to: {physical address}.

Gifts

If the item was marked as a gift when purchased and shipped directly to you, you’ll receive a gift credit for the value of your return. Once the returned item is received, a gift certificate will be mailed to you.

If the item wasn’t marked as a gift when purchased, or the gift giver had the order shipped to themselves to give to you later, we will send a refund to the gift giver and they will find out about your return.

Shipping returns

To return your product, you should mail your product to: {physical address}.

You will be responsible for paying for your own shipping costs for returning your item. Shipping costs are non-refundable. If you receive a refund, the cost of return shipping will be deducted from your refund.

Depending on where you live, the time it may take for your exchanged product to reach you may vary.

If you are returning more expensive items, you may consider using a trackable shipping service or purchasing shipping insurance. We don’t guarantee that we will receive your returned item.

Need help?

Contact us at {email} for questions related to refunds and returns.

item.title: Types of Sources in AI Search Response
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Types of Sources in AI Search Response

Isha Sachdeva

Founder, visble.ai

The era of endless link-clicking is fading. In fact, studies show that over half of Google searches, a figure often cited as high as 60% now result in a zero-click" event. This means that users find their answer directly in the search results.

This highlights why curating the sources behind these AI summaries is critical for brands to maintain their digital presence. 

If you can understand what feeds the AI, you can optimise your content for a new outcome: AI Engine Optimisation (AEO), which runs alongside traditional Search Engine Optimisation (SEO).

AI search engines like AI Overview, ChatGPT, Gemini, and Perplexity, powered by Large Language Models (LLMs), don't just pick the top-ranking link. They issue multiple related searches across diverse subtopics and data sources to construct a comprehensive, synthesised answer.

Let's dive into the core sources these AI systems use and see how you can utilise them to enhance your brand visibility.

Key Takeaways

  • E-E-A-T for Authority: Long-form Blogs and Content Clusters are essential for establishing the deep topical authority that LLMs prioritise.
  • Reviews Earn AI Trust: Third-party Review Sites and Directories function as the critical social proof layer, directly influencing AI-generated sentiment analysis.
  • Data Structure Over Keyword Stuffing: Implementing Schema Markup (FAQ, Product) instantly boosts your content's "AI Answer Readiness Score" by making data extraction effortless.
  • Landing Pages Drive AI Conversions: AI extracts transactional data (features, pricing, USPs) most effectively from highly structured Landing and Product Pages.
  • The GSOV Metric: Brands tracking their Generative Share of Voice (GSOV) across these six source types see a faster increase in brand mentions in AI Overviews.

What is the Role of Different Sources in AI Search

In AI search, the Large Language Model (LLM) reads, extracts, and compiles content from the web to create a valuable response to the user’s query. The sources listed below are the raw materials that AI uses.

Source Type

Elements

AI Search Use

Relevance for AI Search

Blogs 

Online articles, journals, and expert opinions, often updated regularly.

Used for recent information, detailed guides, personal experience (E-E-A-T), and niche topics.

Medium to High (Depends on E-E-A-T and topical depth)

Landing Pages

Standalone web pages created to explain a product, service or value proposition.

Used for product details, service specifics, pricing, and business offerings.

High (Official company information)

Social Media

User-generated content from platforms like X (Twitter), Reddit, YouTube, TikTok, Facebook, and Instagram.

Used for real-time information, public opinion/sentiment, trends, and user-generated reviews/local discovery.

Medium to High (High volume, but variable reliability)

Review Sites

Platforms like Yelp, TripAdvisor, Google Business Profiles, and industry-specific review sites.

Used for local business information, aggregated opinions, and expert insights

High for Trustworthiness and Experience (E-E-A-T)

Directory Sites

Listings of businesses or organisations (e.g., Yellow Pages, industry-specific directories).

Used for basic business facts (Name, Address, Phone-NAP), hours, and categorisation.

Medium to High (Fundamental, foundational data)

Wikipedia 

A massive, collaborative, and generally well-referenced encyclopedia.

Used for broad factual definitions, historical context, and consensus information.

High (Due to broad peer review/citation requirements)

News Sites

Current event reports, investigative journalism, syndicated news (e.g., AP, Reuters), and globally recognised publications.

Used for real-time relevance, verifiable facts on ongoing events, and establishing Authority and Trustworthiness (E-E-A-T) signals.

Medium (Acts as a trust signal for timely/factual queries).

Other

Academic papers, official government websites, e-commerce product pages, etc.

Used for authoritative, domain-specific, or primary source information.

Varies (Highest for official/scholarly sources)

What are the Source Categories Used by AI to Generate Responses?

Blogs

A blog post by an industry expert (with Expertise and Authoritiveness) is highly valued by AI models. A recent analysis by McKinsey of the shift in digital marketing and consumer discovery due to LLMs found that the era of AI search significantly sidelines proprietary content. 

The report suggests that a brand's own website and content (including blog pages) typically make up only 5-10% of the total sources referenced by AI models, which instead draw from a diverse ecosystem including affiliates and user-generated content.

Having a well-structured and informative blog is important. They are often the best place to provide detailed, long-form answers that an AI can easily extract as a summary. It increases the probability of your content being cited.

Landing Pages 

Landing pages provide specific, transactional information to AI-driven search engines. When users ask, "What is the price of X product?" or "What features are included in Y service?" It’s easy to generate an answer from these pages.

Ensuring your landing pages have a clear value proposition, concise headings and properly marked-up data (like product schema and FAQs) helps the AI extract this commercial information accurately.

Social Media (UGC) 

Social media as a source includes User-Generated Content (UGC). AI models search platforms like YouTube, Instagram, and Reddit. In fact, Reddit outranks financial experts 176% of the time when ChatGPT answers finance questions. 

This helps AI get real-world experience, current public sentiment, and local discoveries. For example, an AI might use a Reddit thread to understand a common problem with a product or an Instagram reel to find an affordable cafe. YouTube is especially considered for procedural "how-to" information.

Authentic community engagement is key. AI sees these peer-to-peer conversations as real-world, relevant knowledge. 

Optimising your social profiles and encouraging genuine reviews/discussions becomes a strong signal to the AI about your brand's real-world relevance and reputation.

Review Sites 

Review sites incorporate aggregated platforms for customer feedback and ratings. Google Business Profile, Trustpilot, or Amazon are some of the review sites.

Skimming through reviews, AI models evaluate your brand’s Trustworthiness and Experience. It summarises the overall sentiment or extracts specific common themes from a large volume of reviews to include in a response.

When you go to the review section of Amazon, you must have seen a short paragraph summarising customer feedback before product images and written reviews. That’s also done by AI (e.g., "The fabric is appreciated for the quality, but some users find it overpriced").

Managing your online reputation on these platforms is highly recommended. AI uses them to ground its sentiment and quality checks.

Directory Sites

Websites that list businesses, organisations or people, often categorised by location or industry. Directory sites are like a digital phone book.

Directories are vital for validating Non-Contradictory Fact Checks, especially for local businesses. The AI checks these sites to confirm the business's NAP (Name, Address, Phone), operating hours, and primary services.

Consistent and accurate listings across major directories make AI search engines trust you.

Wikipedia

Wikipedia is a free, online encyclopedia written and maintained by a community of volunteers.

A research study analysed how AI search compares to traditional Google rankings. The study used ChatGPT and the Google AI Model for analysis. Prompts were tested across five major industries. These industries were finance, digital technology, business services, consumer electronics, and fashion. 

The findings are clear: Wikipedia is highly prominent. It was cited as the number one or two source in almost every industry reviewed.

This has led to an 8% drop in human pageviews for Wikipedia. It reflects AI's growing ability to directly answer questions based on its training data, which often includes Wikipedia content.  

Wikipedia provides broad, introductory, and factual consensus information. Its content is highly structured and mostly well-cited, making it easy for AI models to process and rely on for definitions and facts.

While you can't optimise your own site on Wikipedia, being cited by Wikipedia (or having your main entities mentioned there) is a strong authority signal that the AI will recognise.

News and Major Media Outlets

A News and Major Media Outlet refers to a website or digital section dedicated to reporting current events, journalistic investigations, and expert commentary on a wide range of topics, from global politics to market trends. 

The source reflects Trustworthiness and Authoritativeness (E-E-A-T). AI search engines rely on it for real-time relevance and verifiable facts concerning ongoing events. 

Major, globally recognised publications (like The New York Times or Reuters) are consistently among the top-cited domains. 

Being cited acts as a super-charged backlink of trust for AI, significantly boosting your overall digital authority and increasing the probability of your content being chosen by LLMs. This is significant for queries seeking immediate, factual updates.

Other Sources (Scholarly, E-commerce, etc.) 

This includes everything else that’s left. You can consider highly authoritative domains such as government or educational sites (.gov, .edu), academic journals, and product pages on large e-commerce sites.

AI crawls:

  • Scholarly/Official sources for data, statistics, and highly authoritative/verified facts. It reflects Expertise and Authoritativeness.
  • E-commerce for specific product configurations, models, and comparisons.

If your business is featured in a news article or referenced in a scholarly paper, this provides one of the strongest "Trustworthiness" signals to the AI.

How to Track Source Categories with Visble.ai?

Tracking and monitoring top source categories is essential for AEO. Visble.ai, a specialised AI analytics tool, excels at tracking brand citations and domain authority within Generative AI responses.

How it works:

  1. Campaign Creation: The platform allows you to create a campaign using a large volume of prompts and queries related to your target keywords and core topics.
  2. Source Curation: Visble.ai’s algorithm curates an exhaustive list of all the external web sources and domains cited by AI Search Engines (like Google's AI Overviews, or LLMs like Gemini and ChatGPT) to answer those queries.
  3. Categorisation & Analysis: The tool then systematically organises these cited domains into their respective Source Categories (e.g., News, Blog, Directory, Forum, Research). This generates a quantifiable report showing the percentage of involvement for each source category.

With this data, you can clearly see which type of content is most highly valued by AI models for your specific topics. This allows you to strategically focus your content creation efforts on the most appearing source category, directly enhancing your brand’s visibility and citation probability across LLMs.

Conclusion

Since AI has become a common tool for generating content, you will find a lot of AI-generated content published on the internet. AI-generated content may have some misinformation if not responsibly rectified by an industry expert. 

This is where your brand can stand out. Create original and unique content. Provide information, guidance, and advice as an expert for humans, while following the strategies to make AI models like you.

You don’t need to target every source for brand visibility. You have to figure out which sources work best for your business and what metrics to focus on to enhance your Generative Share of Voice.

Landing Pages\u00a0<\/b><\/h3>

Landing pages provide specific, transactional information to AI-driven search engines. When users ask, <\/span>\"What is the price of X product?\" or \"What features are included in Y service?\" It\u2019s easy to generate an answer from these pages.<\/span><\/p>

Ensuring your landing pages have a clear value proposition, concise headings and properly marked-up data (like product schema and FAQs) helps the AI extract this commercial information accurately.<\/span><\/p>

Social Media (UGC)\u00a0<\/b><\/h3>

Social media as a source includes User-Generated Content (UGC). AI models search platforms like YouTube, Instagram, and Reddit. In fact, Reddit outranks financial experts 176% of the time when ChatGPT answers finance questions.\u00a0<\/span><\/p>

This helps AI get real-world experience, current public sentiment, and local discoveries. For example, an AI might use a<\/span> Reddit thread to understand<\/span><\/a> a common problem with a product or an Instagram reel to find an affordable cafe. YouTube is especially considered for procedural \"how-to\" information.<\/span><\/p>

Authentic community engagement is key. AI sees these peer-to-peer conversations as real-world, relevant knowledge.\u00a0<\/span><\/p>

Optimising your social profiles and encouraging genuine reviews\/discussions becomes a strong signal to the AI about your brand's real-world relevance and reputation.<\/span><\/p>

Review Sites\u00a0<\/b><\/h3>

Review sites incorporate aggregated platforms for customer feedback and ratings. Google Business Profile, Trustpilot, or Amazon are some of the review sites.<\/span><\/p>

Skimming through reviews, AI models evaluate your brand\u2019s Trustworthiness and Experience. It summarises the overall sentiment or extracts specific common themes from a large volume of reviews to include in a response.<\/span><\/p>

When you go to the review section of Amazon, you must have seen a short paragraph summarising customer feedback before product images and written reviews. That\u2019s also done by AI (e.g., \"The fabric is appreciated for the quality, but some users find it overpriced\").<\/span><\/p>

Managing your online reputation<\/span><\/a> on these platforms is highly recommended. AI uses them to ground its sentiment and quality checks.<\/span><\/p>

Directory Sites<\/b><\/h3>

Websites that list businesses, organisations or people, often categorised by location or industry. Directory sites are like a digital phone book.<\/span><\/p>

Directories are vital for validating Non-Contradictory Fact Checks, especially for local businesses. The AI checks these sites to confirm the business's NAP (Name, Address, Phone), operating hours, and primary services.<\/span><\/p>

Consistent and accurate listings across major directories make AI search engines trust you.<\/span><\/p>

Wikipedia<\/b><\/h3>

Wikipedia is a free, online encyclopedia written and maintained by a community of volunteers.<\/span><\/p>

A research study analysed how AI search compares to traditional Google rankings. The study used ChatGPT and the Google AI Model for analysis. Prompts were tested across five major industries. These industries were finance, digital technology, business services, consumer electronics, and fashion.\u00a0<\/span><\/p>

The findings are clear: Wikipedia is highly prominent. It was cited as the number one or two source in almost every industry reviewed.<\/span><\/p>

This has led to an <\/span>8% drop in human pageviews<\/span><\/a> for Wikipedia. It reflects AI's growing ability to directly answer questions based on its training data, which often includes Wikipedia content.\u00a0\u00a0<\/span><\/p>

Wikipedia provides broad, introductory, and factual consensus information. Its content is highly structured and mostly well-cited, making it easy for AI models to process and rely on for definitions and facts.<\/span><\/p>

While you can't optimise your own site on Wikipedia, being cited by Wikipedia (or having your main entities mentioned there) is a strong authority signal that the AI will recognise.<\/span><\/p>

News and Major Media Outlets<\/b><\/h3>

A News and Major Media Outlet refers to a website or digital section dedicated to reporting current events, journalistic investigations, and expert commentary on a wide range of topics, from global politics to market trends.\u00a0<\/span><\/p>

The source reflects Trustworthiness and Authoritativeness (E-E-A-T). AI search engines rely on it for real-time relevance and verifiable facts concerning ongoing events.\u00a0<\/span><\/p>

Major, globally recognised publications (like The New York Times or Reuters) are consistently among the top-cited domains.\u00a0<\/span><\/p>

Being cited acts as a super-charged backlink of trust for AI, significantly boosting your overall digital authority and increasing the probability of your content being chosen by LLMs. This is significant for queries seeking immediate, factual updates.<\/span><\/p>

Other Sources (Scholarly, E-commerce, etc.)\u00a0<\/b><\/h3>

This includes everything else that\u2019s left. You can consider highly authoritative domains such as government or educational sites (.gov, .edu), academic journals, and product pages on large e-commerce sites.<\/span><\/p>

AI crawls:<\/span><\/p>

  • Scholarly\/Official sources for data, statistics, and highly authoritative\/verified facts. It reflects Expertise and Authoritativeness.<\/span><\/li>
  • E-commerce for specific product configurations, models, and comparisons.<\/span><\/li><\/ul>

    If your business is featured in a news article or referenced in a scholarly paper, this provides one of the strongest \"Trustworthiness\" signals to the AI.<\/span><\/p>","__globals__":{"link_color":"globals\/colors?id=primary","link_hover_color":"globals\/colors?id=accent"},"typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Manrope","typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"px","size":35,"sizes":[]}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"}],"isInner":true},{"id":"28cbf78","elType":"container","settings":{"content_width":"full","margin":{"unit":"px","top":"0","right":"0","bottom":"0","left":"0","isLinked":false},"_element_id":"seovsgeo","padding_mobile":{"unit":"px","top":"0","right":"20","bottom":"0","left":"20","isLinked":false}},"elements":[{"id":"8670f3a","elType":"widget","settings":{"title":"How to Track Source Categories with Visble.ai?","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Plus Jakarta Sans","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":50,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_tablet":{"unit":"px","size":40,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile_extra":{"unit":"px","size":36,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile":{"unit":"px","size":26,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_weight":"600","typography_line_height":{"unit":"em","size":1.2,"sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_laptop":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_tablet_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_mobile_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"text_stroke_text_stroke_type":"yes","_margin":{"unit":"px","top":"20","right":"0","bottom":"20","left":"0","isLinked":false},"_element_width":"inherit","_css_classes":"txt-gradient"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"e61c429","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

    2. Source Curation: <\/b>Visble.ai<\/span><\/a>\u2019s algorithm curates an exhaustive list of all the external web sources and domains cited by AI Search Engines (like Google's AI Overviews, or LLMs like Gemini and ChatGPT) to answer those queries.<\/span><\/li>
    3. Categorisation & Analysis:<\/b> The tool then systematically organises these cited domains into their respective Source Categories (e.g., News, Blog, Directory, Forum, Research). This generates a quantifiable report showing the percentage of involvement for each source category.<\/span><\/li><\/ol>

      With this data, you can clearly see which type of content is most highly valued by AI models for your specific topics. This allows you to strategically focus your content creation efforts on the most appearing source category, directly enhancing your brand\u2019s visibility and citation probability across LLMs.<\/span><\/p>","__globals__":{"link_color":"globals\/colors?id=primary","link_hover_color":"globals\/colors?id=accent"},"typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Manrope","typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"px","size":35,"sizes":[]}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"},{"id":"30b206b","elType":"container","settings":{"content_width":"full","margin":{"unit":"px","top":"0","right":"0","bottom":"15","left":"0","isLinked":false},"_element_id":"correlationgeo","width_mobile":{"unit":"px","size":321.667},"_flex_size":"none","_element_width_mobile":"initial"},"elements":[{"id":"d5ab5a7","elType":"widget","settings":{"title":"Conclusion ","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Plus Jakarta Sans","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":50,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_tablet":{"unit":"px","size":40,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile_extra":{"unit":"px","size":36,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile":{"unit":"px","size":26,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_weight":"600","typography_line_height":{"unit":"em","size":1.2,"sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_laptop":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_tablet_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_mobile_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"text_stroke_text_stroke_type":"yes","_margin":{"unit":"px","top":"20","right":"0","bottom":"20","left":"0","isLinked":false},"_element_width":"inherit","_css_classes":"txt-gradient"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"9b0edcf","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

Designation If your SEO strategy still revolves around meta tags and backlinks alone, it’s time for a reality check. You can't play by the old-school tactics anymore since Generative Search Experience is rapidly gaining momentum. ChatGPT reached 365 billion annual searches in just two years, 5.5...
Types of Sources in AI Search Response
Let's dive into the core sources these AI systems use and see how you can utilise them to enhance your brand visibility.

Types of Sources in AI Search Response

Isha Sachdeva

Founder, visble.ai

[elementor-element k="6d3c116b80439e24d3e0aa22e213b6e0" data=""]

Key Takeaways

  • E-E-A-T for Authority: Long-form Blogs and Content Clusters are essential for establishing the deep topical authority that LLMs prioritise.
  • Reviews Earn AI Trust: Third-party Review Sites and Directories function as the critical social proof layer, directly influencing AI-generated sentiment analysis.
  • Data Structure Over Keyword Stuffing: Implementing Schema Markup (FAQ, Product) instantly boosts your content's "AI Answer Readiness Score" by making data extraction effortless.
  • Landing Pages Drive AI Conversions: AI extracts transactional data (features, pricing, USPs) most effectively from highly structured Landing and Product Pages.
  • The GSOV Metric: Brands tracking their Generative Share of Voice (GSOV) across these six source types see a faster increase in brand mentions in AI Overviews.

item.title: Visble.ai vs. Otterly.ai: A Comparative Analysis
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Visble.ai vs. Otterly.ai: A Comparative Analysis

Isha Sachdeva
Founder, visble.ai SERP ranks are now obsolete. The user journey now begins with a synthesised answer from an AI, be it Google's AI Overviews, ChatGPT, Gemini, or Perplexity. This shift has created Generative Engine Optimisation (GEO). It’s a new discipline that’s important for marketing, content, and PR teams. If an AI search engine is the first touchpoint, and your brand isn't being cited in the answer, you are invisible to a massive, fast-growing segment of the market. Your traditional SEO toolkit is blind to this reality. To win in the GEO era, you need platforms that track, analyse, and help you optimise for AI’s unique citation logic. Visble.ai and Otterly.ai are two leading platforms defining this new space. While they both aim to monitor brand visibility in AI search, their feature sets and strategic value diverge sharply. Here’s a definitive comparison to provide the expert insight necessary to choose the right command centre for your brand's AI future.

Key Takeaways

  • The SEO to GEO Shift: The user journey has fundamentally changed from optimising for link rank (SEO) to optimising for brand citation (GEO) within AI-generated responses.
  • Two Pillars of GEO: AI visibility is determined by two factors: Discovery (brand mentions/sentiment in high-trust, third-party content) and topical authority (structured, cite-ready on-site data).
  • Otterly.ai is for Monitoring and Technical Fixes: Otterly.ai is an observational tool best suited for prompt monitoring, daily tracking, and technical web audits (AI readiness). Its prompt allowance is a key limitation for large scale analysis of your industry.
  • Visble.ai is for Strategic Control and Optimisation: Visble.ai is built for proactive, goal oriented GEO. It provides strategic control through campaign-based prompt generation, deeper source/trend analysis, and a superior entry-level prompt volume for competitive experimentation.
  • The Buying Decision: Choose Visble.ai for strategic, competitive intelligence and narrative control. Go with Otterly for fast, basic monitoring and immediate technical compliance/audit needs.

The Foundational Challenge: From Ranking to Citing

The core difference between SEO and GEO is the shift from a “ranking” problem to “being cited” problem. AI models rely on two key pillars to determine your visibility:
  1. Discovery (The Narrative): How frequently and positively your brand is mentioned across high-trust, third-party content (e.g., industry news, Reddit, forums).
  2. Topical Authority (The semantics): The accessibility and structured format of your on-site content, making it safe for the LLM to pull facts and data points.
The platform you choose must provide clear paths to address both.
Key Visibility Signal Traditional SEO Focus Generative Engine Optimisation (GEO)
User Query Short-tail keywords (e.g., "best CRM") Conversational Prompts (e.g., "What are the three best CRM solutions for a team of ten?")
Success Metric Click-Through-Rate (CTR) from a #1 link Brand Citation Frequency and Sentiment within the AI Answer
Content Strategy Blogs, guides with keywords Structured data, tables, definitional content, and FAQs

What is Otterly.ai

Otterly.ai is an AI search monitoring platform. It’s focused on Generative Engine Optimisation (GEO) and brand safety. You can track your brand mentions, sentiment, and website citations across leading AI search engines like ChatGPT, Google AI Overviews, and Perplexity on a single prompt level. It is an observational data-gathering tool. A tool that helps you analyse how your brand appears. But there are limited actionable steps beyond that. It's like you get the data and analysis, but the strategic moves to improve are limited in otterly.ai.

What is Visble.ai

Visble.ai is an AI search analytics tool for Answer Engine Optimisation (AEO) that helps you run analysis on a large number of prompts. Going beyond just monitoring, it provides strategy recommendations and data on key content patterns. So, you get the data and actionable steps to maximise your brand’s visibility. The platform guides users on exactly what and where to publish to improve brand positioning in AI-generated responses.

Head-to-Head Feature Comparison: The Expert Matrix

To assess which tool is the right fit, let’s analyse the functional depth of their core features, particularly their approach to analytics, control, and implementation.
Feature Category Visble.ai  Otterly.ai  Strategic Implication
Analysis Granularity Both Aggregated and Individual prompt-level analysis provided. Only prompt-level analysis is provided. Visble.ai enables macro-trend spotting (Aggregated) while maintaining individual detail.   Otterly.ai is limited to a single, specific prompt result.
Campaign Control Campaigns divided according to objective.   Prompts are generated as per the objective, granting user control. Campaigns are created on random prompts; the user lacks control over the prompt choice. A major differentiator. Visble.ai allows alignment with business goals (e.g., "Targeting Mid-Market Segment"), offering genuine strategic GEO execution.
Source & Trend Analysis Detailed source analysis with historical trends is provided. Tracks AI-generated source citations and trends. The core of Authority.   Visble.ai pinpoints which external domains are influencing the AI's opinion of your brand, directing your brand mention, UGC and competitive analysis strategy.
Content Deep Dive Detailed response and topic analysis are provided. No content analysis is provided. Narrative Control.   Visble.ai shows which claims or specific topics from your website were pulled by the LLM, allowing you to refine your on-page messaging for maximum citation.
Web Audit Function Does not provide web audits. Provides Web Audit (focused on AI readiness). Otterly’s implementation strength.   It directly addresses technical GEO readiness, bridging the gap between insight and the actions developers or SEOs need to take on-site.
Reporting Frequency Weekly analysis update (customizable for enterprise). Daily analysis update provided. Otterly.ai provides faster feedback loops, critical in volatile search spaces.   Visble.ai's weekly focus is more suited for high-level strategy and trend identification.
Entry-Level Value Starter Plan: $29/month for 50 prompts, allowing two campaigns.   Business plan: $249/month with 1500 prompts and unlimited campaigns.   Enterprise Plan: Custom Pricing Started Plan: $29/month for 15 prompts.   Standard Plan: $189/month for 100 prompts.   Premium Plan: $489/month for 400 prompts. Visble.ai is the clear winner on volume.   It provides comparatively more prompt tracking for the same price, essential for competitive and bulk experimentation.
Track GEO Metrics Now

What AI Visibility Tool Features Matter for Your Brand

The true value of an AI visibility tool lies in its ability to generate actionable and strategic data. It’s not just about a dashboard of numbers. Let’s analyse Visble.ai and Otterly.ai through specific features.

1. Prompt Control & Objective-Based GEO

The largest gap is in Campaign Control.
  • Visble.ai provides you with a platform where prompts are generated according to the campaign objective. It helps you move from passive monitoring to proactive market shaping.
You don't just track if you're mentioned. You’ll also know if you're mentioned favourably when a user asks a comparison question relevant to your target persona.
  • Otterly.ai’s approach is limited to a random set of prompts with no user control. It severely restricts strategic use. With GEO, if you can’t control the conversation you’re monitoring, you can't align the data with your marketing P&L. So, you lack full control of competitive analysis and product launch tracking.

2. Source and Response Analysis

AI models are trained to synthesise and narrate. They first validate information, then cite it.
  • Visble.ai’s Source & Trend Analysis outperforms Otterly.ai here. It doesn't just show where your brand was mentioned; it tracks the trend of that source's influence on the LLM.
If an obscure forum suddenly becomes a high-citation source for your competitor, Visble.ai will immediately surface this strategic threat. You can then prioritise earning a mention there.
  • The Detailed Response and Topic Analysis provides narrative control. It breaks down the AI’s answer, showing which of your on-page data points or claims were included.
This is the feedback loop for content teams: Did the AI capture our value proposition correctly? If not, what must we restructure? Otterly.ai’s lack of this content analysis leaves the GEO team flying blind on one of the most important aspects of optimisation.

3. Daily Audits vs. Bulk Experimentation

While Visble.ai wins on strategic depth, Otterly.ai offers an invaluable, distinct utility:
  • Otterly.ai’s Web Audit: By auditing structured data and content layout, it addresses the technical shortcomings that often prevent citation, even if the content is excellent. This is an essential, immediate-value feature for any SEO team.
  • Visble.ai’s Volume Edge: The 50 prompt starting point is non-negotiable for competitive work. A typical brand needs to track ~5 core prompts, ~10 high-intent transactional prompts, and ~5 competitors across those prompts.
Otterly’s 15 prompts force an artificial, non-strategic limitation. This hinders the essential bulk experimentation required. Basically, you don’t get to identify which types of prompts yield the highest citation rate.

Strategic Recommendation

The choice between Visble.ai and Otterly.ai ultimately comes down to your team’s size, budget allocation, and, most importantly, your primary strategic objective.

Visble.ai is for you if:

  • You’re an enterprise or strategically-driven brand that needs to tie AI visibility directly to specific marketing objectives.
  • You prioritise competitive intelligence and narrative control through detailed source and response trend analysis.
  • You’re operating with a need for in-depth monitoring and bulk experimentation for optimal value.

Otterly.ai is for you if:

  • You’re a small team or solo SEO & GEO, looking for a quick way for technical compliance and basic AI monitoring.
  • Your team is highly focused on the technical implementation side and needs the integrated web audit tool to fix on-page readiness issues.
  • You’re operating in a market requiring daily reporting for real-time responsiveness.

To Conclude

Visble.ai is a clear choice for those demanding strategic control, deep competitive intelligence, and superior entry-level value. The tool’s features are built to drive proactive, objective-based GEO to enhance your brand’s visibility. However, for the SEO team that needs a tool for immediate audit and monitoring the basics, Otterly.ai is an excellent, action-oriented choice.

SERP ranks are now obsolete. The user journey now begins with a synthesised answer from an AI, be it Google's AI Overviews, ChatGPT, Gemini, or Perplexity.\u00a0<\/span><\/p>

This shift has created Generative Engine Optimisation (GEO). It\u2019s a new discipline that\u2019s important for marketing, content, and PR teams.\u00a0\u00a0<\/span><\/p>

If an AI search engine is the first touchpoint, and your brand isn't being cited in the answer, you are invisible to a massive, fast-growing segment of the market. Your traditional SEO toolkit is blind to this reality.\u00a0<\/span><\/p>

To win in the GEO era, you need platforms that track, analyse, and help you optimise for AI\u2019s unique citation logic. Visble.ai and Otterly.ai are two leading platforms defining this new space. While they both aim to monitor brand visibility in AI search, their feature sets and strategic value diverge sharply.<\/span><\/p>

Here\u2019s a definitive comparison to provide the expert insight necessary to choose the right command centre for your brand's AI The core difference between SEO and GEO is the shift from a \u201cranking\u201d problem to \u201cbeing cited\u201d problem. AI models rely on two key pillars to determine your visibility:<\/span><\/p>

  1. Discovery (The Narrative): How frequently and positively your brand is mentioned across high-trust, third-party content (e.g., industry news, Reddit, forums).<\/span><\/li>
  2. Topical Authority (The semantics): The accessibility and structured format of your on-site content, making it safe for the LLM to pull facts and data points.<\/span><\/li><\/ol>

    The platform you choose must provide clear paths to address both.<\/span><\/p>

    Key Visibility Signal<\/b><\/p><\/td>

    Traditional SEO Focus<\/b><\/p><\/td>

    Generative Engine Optimisation (GEO)<\/b><\/p><\/td><\/tr>

    User Query<\/span><\/p><\/td>

    Short-tail keywords (e.g., \"best CRM\")<\/span><\/p><\/td>

    Conversational Prompts<\/b> (e.g., \"What are the three best CRM solutions for a team of ten?\")<\/span><\/p><\/td><\/tr>

    Success Metric<\/span><\/p><\/td>

    Click-Through-Rate (CTR) from a #1 link<\/span><\/p><\/td>

    Brand Citation Frequency<\/b> and <\/span>Sentiment <\/b>within the AI Answer<\/span><\/p><\/td><\/tr>

    Content Strategy<\/span><\/p><\/td>

    Blogs, guides with keywords<\/span><\/p><\/td>

    Structured data, tables, definitional content, and FAQs<\/span><\/p><\/td><\/tr><\/tbody><\/table>

    It is an observational data-gathering tool. A tool that helps you analyse how your brand appears. But there are limited actionable steps beyond that.     Visble.ai<\/span><\/a> is an AI search analytics tool for Answer Engine Optimisation (AEO) that helps you run analysis on a large number of prompts. Going beyond just monitoring, it provides strategy recommendations and data on key content patterns. So, you get the data and actionable steps to maximise your brand\u2019s visibility.\u00a0<\/span><\/p>

    The platform guides users on exactly what and where to publish to improve brand positioning in AI-generated responses.<\/span><\/p>","__globals__":{"link_color":"globals\/colors?id=primary","link_hover_color":"globals\/colors?id=accent"},"typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Manrope","typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"px","size":35,"sizes":[]}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"},{"id":"127fdbe","elType":"container","settings":{"content_width":"full","margin":{"unit":"px","top":"20","right":"0","bottom":"15","left":"0","isLinked":false}},"elements":[{"id":"4eb25be","elType":"container","settings":{"content_width":"full"},"elements":[{"id":"2581a9b","elType":"widget","settings":{"title":"Head-to-Head Feature Comparison: The Expert Matrix\n","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Plus Jakarta Sans","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":50,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_tablet":{"unit":"px","size":40,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile_extra":{"unit":"px","size":36,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile":{"unit":"px","size":26,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_weight":"600","typography_line_height":{"unit":"em","size":1.2,"sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_laptop":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_tablet_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_mobile_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"text_stroke_text_stroke_type":"yes","_margin":{"unit":"px","top":"20","right":"0","bottom":"20","left":"0","isLinked":false},"_element_width":"inherit","_css_classes":"txt-gradient"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"}],"isInner":true},{"id":"92c80d5","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

    To assess which tool is the right fit, let\u2019s analyse the functional depth of their core features, particularly their approach to analytics, control, and implementation.<\/span><\/p>

    Feature Category<\/b><\/p><\/td>

    Visble.ai\u00a0<\/b><\/p><\/td>

    Otterly.ai\u00a0<\/b><\/p><\/td>

    Strategic Implication<\/b><\/p><\/td><\/tr>

    Analysis Granularity<\/b><\/p><\/td>

    Both Aggregated and Individual prompt-level analysis provided.<\/span><\/p><\/td>

    Only prompt-level analysis is provided.<\/span><\/p><\/td>

    Visble.ai enables macro-trend spotting (Aggregated) while maintaining individual detail.\u00a0<\/span><\/p>

    Otterly.ai is limited to a single, specific prompt result.<\/span><\/p><\/td><\/tr>

    Campaign Control<\/b><\/p><\/td>

    Campaigns divided according to objective.\u00a0<\/span><\/p>

    Prompts are generated as per the objective, granting user control.<\/span><\/p><\/td>

    Campaigns are created on random prompts; the user lacks control over the prompt choice.<\/span><\/p><\/td>

    A major differentiator.\u00a0<\/span><\/p>

    Visble.ai allows alignment with business goals (e.g., \"Targeting Mid-Market Segment\"), offering genuine strategic GEO execution.<\/span><\/p><\/td><\/tr>

    Source & Trend Analysis<\/b><\/p><\/td>

    Detailed source analysis with historical trends is provided.<\/span><\/p><\/td>

    Tracks AI-generated source citations and trends.<\/span><\/p><\/td>

    The core of Authority.\u00a0<\/span><\/p>

    Visble.ai pinpoints which external domains are influencing the AI's opinion of your brand, directing your brand mention, UGC and competitive analysis strategy.<\/span><\/p>

    \u00a0<\/span><\/p><\/td><\/tr>

    Content Deep Dive<\/b><\/p><\/td>

    Detailed response and topic analysis are provided.<\/span><\/p><\/td>

    No content analysis is provided.<\/span><\/p><\/td>

    Narrative Control.<\/span><\/p>

    Visble.ai shows which claims or specific topics from your website were pulled by the LLM, allowing you to refine your on-page messaging for maximum citation.<\/span><\/p><\/td><\/tr>

    Web Audit Function<\/b><\/p><\/td>

    Does not provide web audits.<\/span><\/p><\/td>

    Provides Web Audit (focused on AI readiness).<\/span><\/p><\/td>

    Otterly\u2019s implementation strength.\u00a0<\/span><\/p>

    It directly addresses technical GEO readiness, bridging the gap between insight and the actions developers or SEOs need to take on-site.<\/span><\/p><\/td><\/tr>

    Reporting Frequency<\/b><\/p><\/td>

    Weekly analysis update (customizable for enterprise).<\/span><\/p><\/td>

    Daily analysis update provided.<\/span><\/p><\/td>

    Otterly.ai provides faster feedback loops, critical in volatile search spaces.\u00a0<\/span><\/p>

    Visble.ai's weekly focus is more suited for high-level strategy and trend identification.<\/span><\/p><\/td><\/tr>

    Entry-Level Value<\/b><\/p><\/td>

    Starter Plan: $29\/month for 50 prompts, allowing two campaigns.<\/span><\/p>

    Business plan: $249\/month with 1500 prompts and unlimited campaigns.<\/span><\/p>

    Enterprise Plan: Custom Pricing<\/span><\/p><\/td>

    Started Plan: $29\/month for 15 prompts.<\/span><\/p>

    Standard Plan: $189\/month for 100 prompts.<\/span><\/p>

    Premium Plan: $489\/month for 400 prompts.<\/span><\/p><\/td>

    Visble.ai is the clear winner on volume.<\/span><\/p>

    It provides comparatively more prompt tracking for the same price, essential for competitive and bulk Sans","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":50,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_tablet":{"unit":"px","size":40,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile_extra":{"unit":"px","size":36,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile":{"unit":"px","size":26,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_weight":"600","typography_line_height":{"unit":"em","size":1.2,"sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_laptop":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_tablet_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_mobile_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"text_stroke_text_stroke_type":"yes","_margin":{"unit":"px","top":"20","right":"0","bottom":"20","left":"0","isLinked":false},"_element_width":"inherit","_css_classes":"txt-gradient"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"9b0edcf","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

    The true value of an AI visibility tool lies in its ability to generate actionable and strategic data. It\u2019s not just about a dashboard of numbers. Let\u2019s analyse Visble.ai and Otterly.ai through specific features.<\/span><\/p>

    1. Prompt Control & Objective-Based GEO<\/b><\/h3>

    The largest gap is in Campaign Control.<\/span><\/p>

    • Visble.ai provides you with a platform where prompts are generated<\/span> according to the campaign objective<\/span><\/i>. It helps you move from passive monitoring to proactive market shaping.\u00a0<\/span><\/li><\/ul>

      You don't just track if you're mentioned. You\u2019ll also know if you're mentioned favourably when a user asks a comparison question relevant to your target persona.<\/span><\/p>

      • Otterly.ai\u2019s approach is limited to a random set of prompts with no user control. It severely restricts strategic use. With GEO, if you can\u2019t control the conversation you\u2019re monitoring, you can't align the data with your marketing P&L. So, you lack full control of competitive analysis and product launch tracking.\u00a0<\/span><\/li><\/ul>

        2. Source and Response Analysis<\/b><\/h3>

        AI models are trained to synthesise and narrate. They first validate information, then cite it.<\/span><\/p>

        • Visble.ai\u2019s Source & Trend Analysis outperforms Otterly.ai here. It doesn't just show where your brand was mentioned; it tracks the trend of that source's influence on the LLM.\u00a0<\/span><\/li><\/ul>

          If an obscure forum suddenly becomes a high-citation source for your competitor, Visble.ai will immediately surface this strategic threat. You can then prioritise earning a mention there.<\/span><\/p>

          • The Detailed Response and Topic Analysis provides narrative control. It breaks down the AI\u2019s answer, showing which of your on-page data points or claims were included.\u00a0<\/span><\/li><\/ul>

            This is the feedback loop for content teams: Did the AI capture our value proposition correctly? If not, what must we restructure?\u00a0<\/span><\/p>

            Otterly.ai\u2019s lack of this content analysis leaves the GEO team flying blind on one of the most important aspects of optimisation.
            <\/span><\/p>

            3. Daily Audits vs. Bulk Experimentation<\/b><\/h3>

            While Visble.ai wins on strategic depth, Otterly.ai offers an invaluable, distinct utility:<\/span><\/p>

            • Otterly.ai\u2019s Web Audit:<\/b> By auditing structured data and content layout, it addresses the technical shortcomings that often prevent citation, even if the content is excellent. This is an essential, immediate-value feature for any SEO team.<\/span><\/li>
            • Visble.ai\u2019s Volume Edge: <\/b>The 50 prompt starting point is non-negotiable for competitive work. A typical brand needs to track ~5 core prompts, ~10 high-intent transactional prompts, and ~5 competitors across those prompts.<\/span><\/li><\/ul>

              Otterly\u2019s 15 prompts force an artificial, non-strategic limitation. This hinders the essential bulk experimentation required. Basically, you don\u2019t get to identify which types of prompts yield the highest citation rate.<\/span><\/p>","__globals__":{"link_color":"globals\/colors?id=primary","link_hover_color":"globals\/colors?id=accent"},"typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Manrope","typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"px","size":35,"sizes":[]}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"},{"id":"4b9b7a0","elType":"container","settings":{"content_width":"full","margin":{"unit":"px","top":"0","right":"0","bottom":"15","left":"0","isLinked":false},"_element_id":"musttrackmetrics"},"elements":[{"id":"51b4e30","elType":"widget","settings":{"title":"Strategic Recommendation","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Plus Jakarta Sans","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":50,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_tablet":{"unit":"px","size":40,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile_extra":{"unit":"px","size":36,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile":{"unit":"px","size":26,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_weight":"600","typography_line_height":{"unit":"em","size":1.2,"sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_laptop":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_tablet_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_mobile_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"text_stroke_text_stroke_type":"yes","_margin":{"unit":"px","top":"20","right":"0","bottom":"20","left":"0","isLinked":false},"_element_width":"inherit","_css_classes":"txt-gradient"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"717bfcd","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

Visble.ai vs. Otterly.ai: A Comparative Analysis

Isha Sachdeva

Founder, visble.ai

[elementor-element k="6d3c116b80439e24d3e0aa22e213b6e0" data=""]

Key Takeaways

  • The SEO to GEO Shift: The user journey has fundamentally changed from optimising for link rank (SEO) to optimising for brand citation (GEO) within AI-generated responses.
  • Two Pillars of GEO: AI visibility is determined by two factors: Discovery (brand mentions/sentiment in high-trust, third-party content) and topical authority (structured, cite-ready on-site data).
  • Otterly.ai is for Monitoring and Technical Fixes: Otterly.ai is an observational tool best suited for prompt monitoring, daily tracking, and technical web audits (AI readiness). Its prompt allowance is a key limitation for large scale analysis of your industry.
  • Visble.ai is for Strategic Control and Optimisation: Visble.ai is built for proactive, goal oriented GEO. It provides strategic control through campaign-based prompt generation, deeper source/trend analysis, and a superior entry-level prompt volume for competitive experimentation.
  • The Buying Decision: Choose Visble.ai for strategic, competitive intelligence and narrative control. Go with Otterly for fast, basic monitoring and immediate technical compliance/audit needs.

The Foundational Challenge: From Ranking to Citing

item.title: How to Get Your Website Cited by AI
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
How to Get Your Website Cited by AI

Isha Sachdeva
Founder, visble.ai Between May 2024 and May 2025, overall web crawl traffic (from search engines + AI bots) grew by 18%, while AI-only crawlers have surged, with one major crawler boosting its share from 5% to 30% approximately. In parallel, a 2025 empirical audit of 1,702 citations across AI answer engines found that pages with structured data, semantic HTML, and metadata were consistently more likely to be selected as sources. But many of those crawls never result in user visits. Some sites report nearly 900 bot crawls for every single human referral. So, being crawled is not enough. To get your website noticed and cited by AI, your content must be structured, quotable, and authoritative. That’s where Generative Engine Optimisation (GEO) steps in. Now it’s no longer enough to simply publish and hope for clicks. Your focus must be to excel in Crawlability, Quotability, and Authority. That’s what AI search engines reward.

Technical Accessibility (Ensuring AI Can Read You)

The first step in GEO is ensuring the AI’s "eyes", the LLM-specific crawlers like GPTBot or OAI-Searchbot, can actually access and process your content. Even the highest-quality content is invisible if the bot is blocked.

1. Check Your Host/CDN for AI Blocks

Many popular Content Delivery Networks (CDNs), such as Cloudflare, or certain web hosting providers, have blanket security settings. This automatically blocks known AI crawlers to manage bandwidth or prevent scraping.
  • Action: Contact your hosting provider or review your CDN security settings immediately. Verify that specific AI user agents (e.g., GPTBot, OAI-Searchbot, or newer LLM-specific crawlers) are explicitly allowed access to your website.

2. Configure Your robots.txt Explicitly

Don't rely on implicit rules. While you may want to restrict general crawlers from low-value pages (like login screens), you must proactively grant access to your authoritative content for AI models.
  • Action: Allow specific AI agents to crawl your most valuable sections in your robots.txt file. Example Snippet: User-agent: GPTBot Allow: /blog/ Allow: /research/

3. Reduce JavaScript-Only Content

The Problem: While Googlebot is highly capable of executing client-side JavaScript (JS), many AI crawlers and LLM data pipelines are not. Content rendered dynamically via JS often appears to them as an empty or incomplete page.
  • Action: Prioritise static HTML rendering for all key informational elements; headings, lists, definitions, and core data points. If a fact is important enough to be cited, it must be visible without executing client-side code.

Content Quotability (Making Your Facts Irresistible)

Once the AI can see your site, the next step is to make your content highly extractable. It should be a quotable source material that the LLM prefers to reference over your competitors.

4. The E-E-A-T Foundation: Build Trust for the AI

AI models look for trusted and reliable sources. A fact from a high-E-E-A-T source is what LLMs value.
  • Expert Credentials: Ensure every cited piece has a clear Author Bio with verifiable experience and expertise (the E's in E-E-A-T). Add a link to the author’s profile (LinkedIn, academic journal, professional organisation).
  • Internal Citations: Actively cite and interlink authoritative, third-party sources (research papers, government data, industry reports) within your text. This shows that you have done your homework and anchors your facts in external authority, boosting your page’s credibility score.

5. Format for Instant Extraction: The Direct Answer Rule

AI does not have time for stories. It needs concise, self-contained answers.
  • Action: Adopt a journalistic, AI-friendly writing style. Start every major section or question with a concise, direct, 1-2 sentence answer.
    • Bad: "Before we discuss the steps, let's look at the history of schema..."
    • Good: "Schema Markup is machine code that tells search engines exactly what your content means, making it highly valuable for AI citations."
This "answer-first" structure ensures that if the AI extracts only one sentence, it still captures the core fact, making your content a high-probability quote.

6. Publish Original Data & "Metrics Pieces"

AI prioritises unique, non-duplicative data. If you have the only source for a specific statistic, the AI must cite you to include that data in its summary.
  • Action: Conduct and publish original research, statistics, case studies, or industry surveys.
    • Value: When users prompt, "What are the latest stats on XYZ?" the AI will prefer to cite your unique data over a summary of common knowledge.
Data Insight: Studies show that pages containing exclusive metrics and charts have an average of 2.7% higher cite rate in AI overviews compared to informational articles lacking original data.

Authority Signals (Boosting Your Brand's Trust Score)

This final set of strategies focuses on structural and off-site signals. These are the ones that validate your brand as a trustworthy, well-established entity that search engines rely on.

7. Schema Markup Mastery for Structured Answers

Schema is the direct line to the AI's processing layer. It tells the machine, "This is a question, and this is the answer," streamlining the synthesis process.
  • Action: Implement advanced schema types:
    • HowTo Schema: Essential for step-by-step guides, making it easy for AI to create numbered instructions.
    • FAQ Page Schema: Explicitly label clear question-and-answer pairs, perfect for AI-generated summaries.
    • Entity Schema: Use tags like Organisation and Person to connect your brand and authors to established knowledge graphs.
This structuring can give you a significant citation boost. Structured data markup can significantly increase the content’s citation probability across various AI platforms compared to unstructured content.

8. Off-Site Trust: Optimising Brand Mentions

GEO is not just about your site. It's about your entity across the web. AI models prioritise brands with known, consistent public profiles.
  • Knowledge Panels & Wikipedia: Ensure your company or key entities have a Wikipedia entry and a verified Google Knowledge Panel. These are core, high-trust signals for LLMs.
  • High-Authority Directories: Actively secure placement and manage profiles on authoritative, highly-ranked review sites and industry directories. AI frequently synthesises these reviews for product recommendations.

9. Consistent Messaging (Entity Alignment)

The AI needs to recognise that your website, your social media, and your directory listings all refer to the same authoritative entity. Action: Ensure your brand name, official product names, and core claims are identical across your website, social profiles, and directory listings. Consistency helps the search engines to correctly map all trust signals to a single, authoritative entity.

How Visble.ai Helps Measure Your AI Success

Optimising for AI citation requires specialised measurement. When doing everything systematically, tracking your progress must also be systematic. That’s what Visble.ai is built for. Monitor your brand’s Citation Share. See how often you are cited compared to your competitors in AI Overviews and LLM responses. Identify the exact, long-tail questions and conversational prompts users are asking AI in your niche. Use this data to generate highly relevant, quote-ready content. Analyse which competitors are winning citations, allowing you to reverse-engineer their E-E-A-T and structure choices. Track GEO Metrics Now

Conclusion

Generative Engine Optimisation (GEO) does not put an end to SEO. It's just that now you have to present your brand in a way that it becomes an irreplaceable, authoritative source. By mastering Crawlability, Quotability, and Authority, you transition your brand from being a passive resource to an active source of AI knowledge. Start measuring your brand's AI visibility today. Visble.ai provides the essential analytics to ensure your website is not just seen by users, but trusted and cited by the AI that guides them.

      <\/span>Allow: \/research\/<\/span><\/li><\/ul>

      3. Reduce JavaScript-Only Content<\/b><\/h3>

      The Problem: While Googlebot is highly capable of executing client-side JavaScript (JS), many AI crawlers and LLM data pipelines are not. Content rendered dynamically via JS often appears to them as an empty or incomplete page.<\/span><\/p>

      • Action:<\/b> Prioritise static HTML rendering for all key informational elements; headings, lists, definitions, and core data points. If a fact is important enough to be cited, it must be visible without executing client-side code.<\/span><\/li><\/ul>","__globals__":{"link_color":"globals\/colors?id=primary","link_hover_color":"globals\/colors?id=accent"},"typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Manrope","typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"px","size":35,"sizes":[]}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"}],"isInner":true},{"id":"eedc401","elType":"container","settings":{"content_width":"full","margin":{"unit":"px","top":"0","right":"0","bottom":"15","left":"0","isLinked":false},"_element_id":"scroll-1"},"elements":[{"id":"1e7945c","elType":"widget","settings":{"title":"Content Quotability (Making Your Facts Irresistible)","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Plus Jakarta Sans","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":50,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_tablet":{"unit":"px","size":40,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile_extra":{"unit":"px","size":36,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile":{"unit":"px","size":26,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_weight":"600","typography_line_height":{"unit":"em","size":1.2,"sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_laptop":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_tablet_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_mobile_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"text_stroke_text_stroke_type":"yes","_margin":{"unit":"px","top":"0","right":"0","bottom":"20","left":"0","isLinked":false},"_element_width":"inherit","_css_classes":"txt-gradient","_padding_mobile":{"unit":"px","top":"0","right":"20","bottom":"0","left":"20","isLinked":false}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"dd897d0","elType":"container","settings":{"content_width":"full"},"elements":[{"id":"4c9db58","elType":"container","settings":{"content_width":"full"},"elements":[{"id":"c8b0238","elType":"widget","settings":{"image":{"url":"https:\/\/visble.ai\/wp-content\/uploads\/2025\/12\/Screenshot-2025-12-17-at-12.43.30-PM.png","id":1842,"size":"","alt":"Content Quotability","source":"library"}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"image"}],"isInner":true}],"isInner":true},{"id":"41e24b9","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

        Once the AI can see your site, the next step is to <\/span>make your content highly extractable.<\/span><\/a> It should be a quotable source material that the LLM prefers to reference over your competitors.<\/span><\/p>

        4. The E-E-A-T Foundation: Build Trust for the AI<\/b><\/h3>

        AI models look for trusted and reliable sources. A fact from a high-E-E-A-T source is what LLMs value.<\/span><\/p>

        • Expert Credentials:<\/b> Ensure every cited piece has a clear Author Bio with verifiable experience and expertise (the E's in E-E-A-T). Add a link to the author\u2019s profile (LinkedIn, academic journal, professional organisation).<\/span><\/li>
        • Internal Citations:<\/b> Actively cite and interlink authoritative, third-party sources (research papers, government data, industry reports) within your text. This shows that you have done your homework and anchors your facts in external authority, boosting your page\u2019s credibility score.\u00a0<\/span><\/li><\/ul>

          5. Format for Instant Extraction: The Direct Answer Rule<\/b><\/h3>

          AI does not have time for stories. It needs concise, self-contained answers.<\/span><\/p>

          • Action: <\/b>Adopt a journalistic, AI-friendly writing style. Start every major section or question with a concise, direct, 1-2 sentence answer.<\/span>
            • Bad: \"Before we discuss the steps, let's look at the history of schema...\"<\/span><\/li>
            • Good: \"Schema Markup is machine code that tells search engines exactly what your content means, making it highly valuable for AI citations.\"<\/span><\/li><\/ul><\/li><\/ul>

              This \"answer-first\" structure ensures that if the AI extracts only one sentence, it still captures the core fact, making your content a high-probability quote.<\/span><\/p>

              6. Publish Original Data & \"Metrics Pieces\"<\/b><\/h3>

              AI prioritises unique, non-duplicative data. If you have the only source for a specific statistic, the AI must cite you to include that data in its summary.<\/span><\/p>

              • Action:<\/b> Conduct and publish original research, statistics, case studies, or industry surveys.<\/span>
                • Value:<\/b> When users prompt, \"What are the latest stats on XYZ?\" the AI will prefer to cite your unique data over a summary of common knowledge.<\/span><\/li><\/ul><\/li><\/ul>

                  Data Insight:<\/b> Studies show that pages containing exclusive metrics and charts have an average of 2.7% higher cite rate in AI overviews compared to informational articles lacking original data.<\/span>

                  <\/p>","__globals__":{"link_color":"globals\/colors?id=primary","link_hover_color":"globals\/colors?id=accent"},"typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Manrope","typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"px","size":35,"sizes":[]},"_padding_mobile":{"unit":"px","top":"0","right":"20","bottom":"0","left":"20","isLinked":false}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"}],"isInner":true},{"id":"74a5e67","elType":"container","settings":{"content_width":"full","margin":{"unit":"px","top":"0","right":"0","bottom":"15","left":"0","isLinked":false},"_element_id":"generativeengine","padding_mobile":{"unit":"px","top":"0","right":"20","bottom":"0","left":"20","isLinked":false}},"elements":[{"id":"cb9ff8f","elType":"widget","settings":{"title":"Authority Signals (Boosting Your Brand's Trust Score)","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Plus Jakarta Sans","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":50,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_tablet":{"unit":"px","size":40,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile_extra":{"unit":"px","size":36,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile":{"unit":"px","size":26,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_weight":"600","typography_line_height":{"unit":"em","size":1.2,"sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_laptop":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_tablet_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_mobile_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"text_stroke_text_stroke_type":"yes","_margin":{"unit":"px","top":"20","right":"0","bottom":"20","left":"0","isLinked":false},"_element_width":"inherit","_css_classes":"txt-gradient"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"4a015f4","elType":"container","settings":{"content_width":"full"},"elements":[{"id":"8605479","elType":"widget","settings":{"image":{"url":"https:\/\/visble.ai\/wp-content\/uploads\/2025\/12\/Screenshot-2025-12-17-at-12.46.44-PM.png","id":1843,"size":"","alt":"Schema Markup","source":"library"}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"image"}],"isInner":true},{"id":"95f0b45","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

                  This final set of strategies focuses on structural and off-site signals. These are the ones that validate your brand as a trustworthy, well-established entity that search engines rely on.<\/span><\/p>

                  7. Schema Markup Mastery for Structured Answers<\/b><\/h3>

                  Schema is the direct line to the AI's processing layer. It tells the machine, \"This is a question, and this is the answer,\" streamlining the synthesis process.<\/span><\/p>

                  • Action: <\/b>Implement advanced schema types:<\/span>
                    • HowTo Schema:<\/b> Essential for step-by-step guides, making it easy for AI to create numbered instructions.<\/span><\/li>
                    • FAQ Page Schema:<\/b> Explicitly label clear question-and-answer pairs, perfect for AI-generated summaries.<\/span><\/li>
                    • Entity Schema:<\/b> Use tags like Organisation and Person to connect your brand and authors to established knowledge graphs.<\/span><\/li><\/ul><\/li><\/ul>

                      This structuring can give you a significant citation boost. Structured data markup can significantly<\/span> increase the content\u2019s citation probability<\/span><\/a> across various AI platforms compared to unstructured content.<\/span><\/p>

                      8. Off-Site Trust: Optimising Brand Mentions<\/b><\/h3>

                      GEO is not just about your site. It's about your entity across the web. AI models prioritise brands with known, consistent public profiles.<\/span><\/p>

                      • Knowledge Panels & Wikipedia:<\/b> Ensure your company or key entities have a Wikipedia entry and a verified Google Knowledge Panel. These are core, high-trust signals for LLMs.<\/span><\/li>
                      • High-Authority Directories:<\/b> Actively secure placement and manage profiles on authoritative, highly-ranked review sites and industry directories. AI frequently synthesises these reviews for product recommendations.<\/span><\/li><\/ul>

                        9. Consistent Messaging (Entity Alignment)<\/b><\/h3>

                        The AI needs to recognise that your website, your social media, and your directory listings all refer to the same authoritative entity.<\/span><\/p>

                        Action:<\/b> Ensure your brand name, official product names, and core claims are identical across your website, social profiles, and directory listings. Consistency helps the search engines to correctly map all trust signals to a single, authoritative entity.<\/span><\/p>","__globals__":{"link_color":"globals\/colors?id=primary","link_hover_color":"globals\/colors?id=accent"},"typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Manrope","typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"px","size":35,"sizes":[]}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"}],"isInner":true},{"id":"28cbf78","elType":"container","settings":{"content_width":"full","margin":{"unit":"px","top":"0","right":"0","bottom":"0","left":"0","isLinked":false},"_element_id":"seovsgeo","padding_mobile":{"unit":"px","top":"0","right":"20","bottom":"0","left":"20","isLinked":false}},"elements":[{"id":"8670f3a","elType":"widget","settings":{"title":"How Visble.ai Helps Measure Your AI Success","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Plus Jakarta Sans","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":50,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_tablet":{"unit":"px","size":40,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile_extra":{"unit":"px","size":36,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile":{"unit":"px","size":26,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_weight":"600","typography_line_height":{"unit":"em","size":1.2,"sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_laptop":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_tablet_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_mobile_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"text_stroke_text_stroke_type":"yes","_margin":{"unit":"px","top":"20","right":"0","bottom":"20","left":"0","isLinked":false},"_element_width":"inherit","_css_classes":"txt-gradient"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"e61c429","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

                        Optimising for AI citation requires specialised measurement. When doing everything systematically, tracking your progress must also be systematic. That\u2019s what <\/span>Visble.ai<\/span><\/a> is built for.<\/span><\/p>","__globals__":{"link_color":"globals\/colors?id=primary","link_hover_color":"globals\/colors?id=accent"},"typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Manrope","typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"px","size":35,"sizes":[]}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"},{"id":"7722b54","elType":"widget","settings":{"image":{"url":"https:\/\/visble.ai\/wp-content\/uploads\/2025\/12\/Screenshot-2025-12-17-at-12.55.25-PM.png","id":1845,"size":"","alt":"Citation Tracking","source":"library"},"caption_source":"custom","caption":"Citation Tracking"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"image"},{"id":"127fdbe","elType":"container","settings":{"content_width":"full","margin":{"unit":"px","top":"20","right":"0","bottom":"15","left":"0","isLinked":false}},"elements":[{"id":"92c80d5","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

                        Monitor your brand\u2019s Citation Share.                         Generative Engine Optimisation (GEO) does not put an end to SEO.
