                    It builds credibility & authority in the industry, where users are actively looking for answers. With more qualified visitors to your site, you ultimately achieve more conversions.<\/p>

                    Let\u2019s dive right into the steps:\u00a0<\/span><\/p>

                    Step 1- Analyse the Influencing Factors<\/strong><\/h3>

                    You need a good understanding of the landscape before you try to compete in AI-powered search. Here\u2019s how to get started:<\/span><\/p>

                    Specify Your Industry & the Right Topics<\/b><\/p>

                    Begin with broad terms before narrowing down to the most important topics or questions. While researching your industry, consider the kinds of questions that consumers would ask an AI assistant, starting at the top of the funnel & moving down the funnel.<\/span><\/p>

                    Spot the Key Players<\/b><\/p>

                    To find out which brands or publications show up most often in AI responses, use <\/span>v<\/span>isble.ai<\/span> Generative Engine Optimization tool<\/a>\u00a0to perform an analysis on hundreds of searches. Unexpected sources, such as specialised blogs or industry newsletters, can end up with more AI citations than well-known media sites.<\/span><\/p>

                    Study the Winning Content Formats<\/b><\/p>

                    Learn which kinds of content is most often mentioned by different AI models, whether it is in-depth how-to manuals, product comparison tables, or expert Q&As. You can direct your content strategy by learning the successful types.<\/span><\/p>

                    Keep Tabs on Your Results to Find Out Trends<\/b><\/p>

                    Instead of manually logging your results in a spreadsheet, use Visible.ai to simplify the entire process.<\/span><\/p>

                    With Visible.ai, you can:<\/span><\/p>

                    • Run large scale prompt experiments across different AI models (like ChatGPT, Gemini, and more)<\/span><\/li>
                    • Create & manage multiple campaigns<\/span><\/li>
                    • Track which sources, brands, as well as content formats are being cited<\/span><\/li>
                    • Monitor trends & performance over time<\/span><\/li><\/ul>

                      Once you\u2019ve tracked a solid batch of prompts, look for patterns. This helps you identify which type of content consistently appear, as well as some unexpected brands that dominate specific topics.<\/span><\/p>

                      By the time you finish this step, you will have a good idea of who\u2019s dominating AI responses or what kinds of content is performing well. You identify where your brand has an opportunity to make a difference. This research becomes the foundation of your entire GEO strategy.<\/span><\/p>

                      This research becomes the foundation of your entire GEO strategy!<\/span><\/p>

                      Step 2- Make Your Content AI Search-Friendly<\/strong><\/h3>

                      Now that you\u2019ve done your homework in Step 1, it's time to start building the kind of content that gets picked up by AI-powered search tools. Think of this step as creating AI-ready content that is structured & super easy for AI models to cite in their responses.<\/span><\/p>

                      AI Loves Structure, Pay Attention to Clarity & Structure<\/b><\/p>

                      AI models prefer organized, easy-to-digest information. So give them:<\/span><\/p>

                      • Clear headings & subheadings<\/span><\/li>
                      • Bullet points & numbered lists<\/span><\/li>
                      • FAQ sections & step-by-step guides<\/span><\/li>
                      • Tables like Pros & Cons, Feature Comparisons, or Pricing Breakdowns<\/span><\/li>
                      • Semantic headings & structure<\/span><\/li><\/ul>

                        The more structured it is, the easier it is for AI to reference. Hence, it is more likely that your brand will show up in its answers.<\/span><\/p>

                        Create Comparison-Ready Content<\/b><\/p>

                        Help the AI by creating side-by-side comparisons or detailed breakdowns that answer user questions.<\/span><\/p>

                        Add Unique Insights & Expert Input<\/b><\/p>

                        Don\u2019t just repeat what everyone else is saying. Add something unique to your content.<\/span><\/p>

                        • Pull in quotes or perspectives from your internal experts.<\/span><\/li>
                        • Share proprietary data or original research.<\/span><\/li>
                        • Include real customer examples, tips, or case studies.<\/span><\/li>
                        • Explain what your brand does differently & why it matters.<\/span><\/li><\/ul>

                          This gives AI models something original & authoritative to help you stand out where everyone else is the same.<\/span><\/p>

                          Speak the AI\u2019s Language (Plain & Precise)<\/b><\/p>

                          In fact, the clearer your language, the better.<\/span><\/p>

                          • Avoid buzzwords & vague phrases.<\/span><\/li>
                          • Use specific terms, especially for products, services, or features.<\/span><\/li>
                          • Consider how a user would phrase a question, and try to answer it in their language.<\/span><\/li><\/ul>

                            By the end of this step, you should have a library of structured as well as citation-friendly content. This type of content is easily understood & referenced by AI in its answers.<\/span><\/p>

                            Step 3- Smart Distribution for Maximum AI Visibility<\/strong><\/h3>

                            You\u2019ve created structured, high-quality content that\u2019s built for AI. Now what?<\/span><\/p>

                            It\u2019s time to distribute your content across trusted citations that generative engines pull from.<\/span><\/p>

                            The Secret Sources Behind AI Citations<\/b><\/p>

                            Here\u2019s a secret: not all citations come from big-name media outlets. AI models often reference niche sites, Reddit threads, or industry-specific forums. Now, reach out to them. Pitch a guest article or fresh data that adds value.\u00a0<\/span><\/p>

                            The goal is to get your voice on their platform, as AI already views them as trustworthy.<\/span><\/p>

                            Use PR to Earn AI Trust<\/b><\/p>

                            Good old PR still works. If you land features or quotes in high-authority publications, that content becomes part of the training data or reference set for AI models. Sponsored content or strategic partnerships on high-traffic industry blogs can give you a strong edge.<\/span><\/p>

                            Don\u2019t Forget Your Channels<\/b><\/p>

                            While third-party credibility is powerful, don\u2019t ignore your website. It\u2019s your direct line to AI engines.\u00a0<\/span><\/p>

                            Make sure your blog or knowledge base:<\/span><\/p>

                            • It is well-structured & easy to crawl.<\/span><\/li>
                            • Uses schema markup where possible.<\/span><\/li>
                            • Gets updated regularly with relevant content.<\/span><\/li>
                            • Answers real-world prompts users might ask.<\/span><\/li><\/ul>

                              Sometimes, AI will reference your content directly if it trusts your site enough.<\/span><\/p>

                              By the end of this step, you\u2019ll have a distribution plan that helps place your AI-optimized content in sources already favoured by AI models. The more often you appear in trusted outlets, the more likely you are to get cited in generative responses.<\/span><\/p>

                              Step 4- Technical GEO- Make Your Site AI-Ready<\/strong><\/h3>

                              Now it\u2019s time to make sure the technical foundation of your website supports all that great work. Think of this step as setting up the backstage so that AI engines can easily find, understand, &\u00a0 trust your content.<\/span><\/p>

                              Make Your Site Easy to Crawl & Index<\/b><\/h4>

                              AI engines rely on web crawlers to discover content. If your site is hard to navigate or slow to load, AI models might miss or avoid your pages. Here\u2019s what to focus on:<\/span><\/p>

                              • Fast loading speeds because nobody likes to wait, especially AI crawlers scanning tons of pages.<\/span><\/li>
                              • Use logical menus, internal links, as well as clear URL structures to prevent bots from getting lost.<\/span><\/li>
                              • AI engines prioritize mobile usability since most users search on their phones.<\/span><\/li><\/ul>

                                Use Schema Markup to Speak AI\u2019s Language<\/b><\/p>

                                Schema markup<\/span><\/a> is like adding metadata tags to your pages that help AI understand exactly what your content is about. It instructs the AI engine to determine whether a page is a product, FAQ, or something else. This extra clarity boosts your chances of being cited accurately.<\/span><\/p>

                                • Implement structured data using JSON-LD (the recommended format).<\/span><\/li>
                                • Prioritize schema for your most important pages, like product pages, blog posts, or FAQs.<\/span><\/li><\/ul>

                                  Implement an LLMS.txt File for Better AI Visibility<\/b><\/p>

                                  One of the newer, powerful tools to guide AI models is the <\/span>LLMS.txt file<\/span><\/a>. Think of it as a roadmap specifically designed for language models & generative AI to better understand your website.\u00a0<\/span><\/p>

                                  Here are some best practices to create an effective LLMS.txt!<\/span><\/p>

                                  Keep it simple<\/b><\/p><\/td>

                                  Use clear, concise language with a clean Markdown structure so AI can easily parse it.<\/span><\/p><\/td><\/tr>

                                  Provide a summary<\/b><\/p><\/td>

                                  Begin with a brief description of your website\u2019s purpose to provide context.<\/span><\/p><\/td><\/tr>

                                  List key sections<\/b><\/p><\/td>

                                  Highlight important areas, such as Services, Blog, & Documentation, with brief explanations.<\/span><\/p><\/td><\/tr>

                                  Use descriptive links<\/b><\/p><\/td>

                                  Link to main pages only, not every single post, as well as describe why those pages matter.<\/span><\/p><\/td><\/tr>

                                  Ensure AI readability<\/b><\/p><\/td>

                                  Use plain text formatting, avoid fancy styling, and use straight quotes (\u2018 or \u2019) instead of curly ones to prevent encoding issues.<\/span><\/p><\/td><\/tr>

                                  Update regularly<\/b><\/p><\/td>

                                  As your site evolves, keep your LLMS.txt current to maintain AI accuracy & relevance.<\/span><\/p><\/td><\/tr>

                                  Place it in the root directory<\/b><\/p><\/td>

                                  This makes it easily discoverable by AI engines scanning your site.<\/span><\/p><\/td><\/tr><\/tbody><\/table>

                                  Adding an LLMS.txt file provides AI models with a clearer understanding of your site\u2019s structure & content priorities, making it easier for them to surface your brand in relevant generative responses.<\/span><\/p>

                                  Ensure Your Site is Secure & Trustworthy<\/b><\/h4>

                                  Security matters, not just for your users but also for AI engines. Having HTTPS (SSL certificates) signals that your site is safe, boosting trust & crawl priority.<\/span><\/p>

                                  By the end of Step 4, your website will be technically optimized for AI discovery & citation, leading the way for stronger visibility as well as authority in generative search results.<\/span><\/p>

                                  Step 5- Measure the outcome<\/strong><\/h3>

                                  Now comes the essential part- tracking your impact! It is vital to measure the performance of your content in AI responses. Let\u2019s see how to do that.<\/span><\/p>

                                  Start with the Right Tools<\/b><\/p>

                                  Make use of AI visibility tracking tools<\/a> to help you monitor how often, where, or in what context your brand shows up in AI-generated answers.<\/span><\/p>

                                  Here are a few metrics you\u2019ll want to keep track of.<\/span><\/p>

                                  • Visibility Score- <\/b>Think of this as your overall AI presence rating.<\/span><\/li>
                                  • Brand Mentions- <\/b>Track how many times your brand or products are getting mentioned by AI.<\/span><\/li>
                                  • Citation Sources- <\/b>See who is referencing you & why.<\/span><\/li><\/ul>

                                    Track Before & After Changes<\/b><\/p>

                                    Every time you publish a new piece of content, especially a well-structured guide or comparison, monitor the effect.<\/span><\/p>

                                    Spot the Gaps<\/b><\/p>

                                    If you are not mentioned in the answers to specific prompts, it shows you where to focus next. Search those prompts to analyze & turn missed opportunities into content ideas that align directly with user intent.<\/span><\/p>

                                    By the end of this step, you\u2019ll have a live dashboard or simple report that gives you a clear view of your performance in AI answers. These insights let you make strategic moves instead of playing guessing games.<\/span><\/p>

                                    Step 6- Iterate for better performance<\/strong><\/h3>

                                    GEO isn\u2019t a set-it-and-forget-it game!<\/span><\/i><\/p>

                                    The AI world is constantly evolving, and the brands that succeed are those that adapt. Here is how to do that.<\/span><\/p>

                                    Scale What\u2019s Working, Repeat Your Wins<\/b><\/p>

                                    If something\u2019s clicking, run with it. Figure out what\u2019s gaining traction, then scale those wins.<\/span><\/p>

                                    Let Go of What\u2019s Not<\/b><\/p>

                                    Not every content format will hit. If your long-form pieces never get cited, consider creating something more structured, such as a how-to guide or a side-by-side comparison. The key is learning, not clinging.<\/span><\/p>

                                    Keep Tabs on the Bigger Picture<\/b><\/p>

                                    New AI models & answer engines are popping up all the time. If you're only optimizing for one model, you could miss out on growing ecosystems that your audience is already using.<\/span>
                                    <\/span>Every few weeks, revisit the landscape & make adjustments as needed.<\/span><\/p>

                                    Treat It Like a Living Strategy<\/b><\/p>

                                    What works today might not work six months from now. AI models are updated, or new content types emerge. So, try monthly check-ins or ongoing content audits to help you stay proactive, not reactive.<\/span><\/p>

                                    Over time, you\u2019ll develop a repeatable feedback loop. It becomes a system that constantly improves your brand\u2019s visibility in AI responses.<\/span><\/p>

                                    Think of it as your brand\u2019s real-time reputation engine for AI search.<\/span><\/p>","__globals__":{"link_color":"globals\/colors?id=primary","link_hover_color":"globals\/colors?id=accent"},"typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Manrope","typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"px","size":35,"sizes":[]}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"},{"id":"a8ef576","elType":"container","settings":{"content_width":"full","margin":{"unit":"px","top":"0","right":"0","bottom":"30","left":"0","isLinked":false}},"elements":[{"id":"9e97f64","elType":"widget","settings":{"title":"The Final Thoughts","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Plus Jakarta Sans","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":50,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_tablet":{"unit":"px","size":40,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile_extra":{"unit":"px","size":36,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile":{"unit":"px","size":26,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_weight":"600","typography_line_height":{"unit":"em","size":1.2,"sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_laptop":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_tablet_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_mobile_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"text_stroke_text_stroke_type":"yes","_margin":{"unit":"px","top":"20","right":"0","bottom":"20","left":"0","isLinked":false},"_element_width":"inherit","_css_classes":"txt-gradient"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"f8d8ff9","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

It's time to upgrade your SEO playbook to meet your audience where they\u2019re searching, due to AI.<\/span><\/p>","__globals__":{"link_color":"globals\/colors?id=primary","link_hover_color":"globals\/colors?id=accent"},"typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Manrope","typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"px","size":35,"sizes":[]}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"}],"isInner":true}],"isInner":true}],"isInner":true}],"isInner":true}],"isInner":true}],"isInner":true}],"isInner":true}],"isInner":true}],"isInner":true},{"id":"2f5dcf3","elType":"container","settings":{"flex_direction":"row","content_width":"full","width":{"unit":"%","size":25,"sizes":[]},"flex_align_items":"stretch","flex_wrap":"wrap","background_background":"classic","background_image":{"url":"","id":"","size":"","alt":"","source":"library"},"background_position":"center item.title: What is Query Fan Out and How to Use it to Improve AI Search Visibility?
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
What is Query Fan Out and How to Use it to Improve AI Search Visibility?

Isha Sachdeva

Founder, visble.ai

If you are still relying solely on traditional SEO strategies, you are optimising for a version of the internet that is rapidly fading out.

While SEO isn't dead, the rules of visibility have fundamentally changed. To capture attention in 2026, you must adapt to Generative Engine Optimisation (GEO). Recent data underscores this shift: pages optimised for the AI retrieval process are 161% more likely to be cited in AI Overviews.

At the core of this new ecosystem is a mechanism called Query Fan Out. This guide discusses exactly what Query Fan Out is and provides a blueprint on how to use it to dominate visibility in Google AI Overviews, ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Gemini.

Highlights

  • Abandon Linear Keywords: Focus on user intent and topic clusters rather than single keywords.
  • Optimise for "Background" Search: Remember that AI Mode runs live queries on Shopping Graphs and Finance data; ensure your schema and data feeds are up-to-date.
  • Structure is Key: Use tables, bullet points, and clear headings to help AI parse your data.
  • Own the Data: Unique, proprietary data is the best defence against being ignored by AI.

Think in Entities: Connect your main topic to all related attributes and concepts to cover the full "Fan Out" spectrum.

What is Query Fan Out in AI Search?

Traditional SEO gave a list of results to user queries based on keyword matching. Query Fan Out puts an end to this redundant technique. It’s an advanced information retrieval technique used by LLMs and AI search engines. 

When a user asks with a complex prompt, the AI does not just search for those specific words. Instead, it "fans out" the request, breaking down the single query into multiple, distinct sub-queries (or sub-intents) to gather a complete picture.

For instance, you search, "What is the best CRM software for a small real estate business?"

  • Traditional Search: Looks for pages containing keywords like "best CRM for real estate," "real estate CRM," and "real estate small business."
  • AI Search with Query Fan Out: The AI reasons that to answer this effectively, it must answer several unasked questions. It splits the main query into sub-queries such as:
    • What are the top CRM features for realtors?
    • Price comparison of CRM tools for small teams.
    • Integration capabilities of real estate CRMs with listing sites.
    • User reviews for Salesforce vs. HubSpot for real estate.

The AI executes these searches in parallel, retrieves the data, and synthesises it into a single, cohesive answer (like an AI Overview). If your content answers one of these "fanned out" sub-queries, even if you don't rank #1 for the main keyword, you have a high probability of being cited.

How Query Fan Out Works in Google’s "AI Mode" and Deep Search?

While Query Fan Out is a general concept in LLMs, it is the specific engine behind Google’s AI Mode. According to recent insights from Google’s VP of Product, Robby Stein, AI Mode uses this technique to act as an agent on your behalf.

When you engage AI Mode or Deep Search, the system doesn't just look for an answer; it conducts a live research session.

  • Real-Time Data Access: Unlike standard LLMs that rely on training data, AI Mode fans out queries to real-time systems. It taps into the Shopping Graph (which updates 2 billion times per hour) and Google Finance to answer sub-queries about price, stock availability, and market trends instantly.
  • Scale of Execution: For complex intents, features like Deep Search can trigger dozens or even hundreds of background queries in seconds.
  • For example, if you search "Plan a 3-day trip to Goa for a bachelorette party," AI Mode fans this out into distinct research paths:
    • Restaurants with group seating (checking reservations via Maps data).
    • Live music venues with high safety ratings.
    • Hotels with suites available for specific dates.
    • Weather forecasts for packing tips.

It then aggregates these data points into a single, cohesive itinerary. To be visible here, your content must be accessible to these specific "background" searches.

How Does the Query Fan-Out Mechanism Work?

The Query Fan Out mechanism typically operates in four distinct stages within a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) framework.

1. Intent Decomposition and Reasoning

You ask a question, and the LLM starts operating as a reasoning engine. It analyses the semantic intent behind the query with the words or terms used. 

It asks: What does the user actually need to know to be satisfied? It identifies entities (e.g., "CRM," "Real Estate") and attributes (e.g., "Price," "Features") and generates a "concept map" of necessary information.

2. Parallel Sub-Query Execution

This is the "Fan Out" phase. The system generates 5, 10, or even 20 specific search queries based on the breakdown of the query. These are sent simultaneously to the search index or knowledge graph. 

This is why AI search is "agentive". It acts as a research assistant performing multiple searches in milliseconds.

3. Cross-Source Verification and Retrieval

The AI retrieves passages, not just whole pages, that answer these specific sub-queries. It prioritises sources with high EEAT (Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, and Trustworthiness) to minimise hallucinations. It looks for consensus across multiple high-authority domains.

4. Synthesis and Citation

Finally, the LLM aggregates the retrieved text chunks. It writes a unique answer for your query, citing the sources that provided the specific data points for each sub-query. Your goal in GEO is to be the source for at least one of these data points.

What Are the Strategic Benefits and Impacts of Query Fan Out?

Shifting your strategy to accommodate Query Fan Out offers distinct competitive advantages that traditional SEO cannot match.

Bypassing the "Top 10" Barrier

The most significant impact of Query Fan Out is that you don’t need to be on the first page of SERPs to get cited. About 68% of citations in AI Overviews come from pages that do not rank in the top 10 for the main query.

Benefit: You no longer need to compete with the giants (like Wikipedia or Amazon) for the main keyword. If you have the best specific answer for a fanned-out sub-query (e.g., specific durability data), the AI must cite you to complete its answer.

Significantly Increased Citation Odds

Optimising for breadth and sub-topics pays off. Pages that rank for fanned-out queries are 161% more likely to be cited in an AI Overview.

Benefit: By covering the "long tail" of a topic exhaustively, you triple your chances of appearing in the coveted zero-position, driving high-intent traffic that has already been qualified by the AI.

Authority Through "Topic Ownership"

Fan Out queries are volatile; they change based on user context. Chasing every single sub-query is impossible. However, sites that demonstrate Topical Authority, covering a subject so thoroughly that they answer most potential fan-outs, win consistently.

Benefit: More resilient brand presence that dominates the Knowledge Graph, ensuring you are the "go-to" entity for the AI.

How Can You Identify Potential Fan Out Opportunities?

You cannot see the exact sub-queries an AI generates, but you can reverse-engineer them. To optimise for Query Fan Out, you must anticipate the AI's reasoning path.

1. Map the Entity-Attribute Matrix

Stop thinking in keywords and start thinking in Entities. An entity is a distinct concept (person, place, thing, concept). AI understands the world through the relationships between entities and their attributes.

  • Entity: "iPhone 16"
  • Attributes: Battery life, Camera specs, Price, Release date, and Colour options.

For every core topic you cover, list every possible attribute a user might care about. The AI will likely fan out queries for each of these attributes.

2. Leverage "People Also Ask" and Auto-Complete

Google's "People Also Ask" boxes are primitive versions of Query Fan Out. They literally show you the related questions Google associates with a topic. If a People Also Ask question exists, you can bet the AI will fan out a sub-query for it.

3. Use AI to Simulate Fan Out

Use the AI against itself. Input your target keyword into ChatGPT or Gemini and ask: "Act as a search engine logic bot. Break down the query '[Your Keyword]' into 10 distinct sub-queries you would need to run to provide a comprehensive, expert answer. Focus on user intent and specific data points." The output will be your content roadmap.

How toStructure Content for Maximum AI Readability?

Generative Engine Optimisation is heavily dependent on formatting. Even if you have the best information, if the AI cannot read it easily, it will ignore it. You need to structure your content so it is crawlable by search engines, along with being readable by humans. 

Use the "Inverted Pyramid" for Sections

For every sub-topic (which corresponds to a sub-query), answer the question directly in the first sentence. Follow with supporting data, and then context. This style allows the AI to easily "grab" the answer snippet.

Implement Explicit Headings and Lists

Avoid vague and generic headings written just to add keywords. Use descriptive and entity-rich headings, that too, in question format.

  • Poor Heading: "Battery Information iPhone 16"
  • Good Heading: "How Long Does the iPhone 16 Battery Last?"

Use bullet points and tables for data. AI models are trained to extract structured data from tables highly efficiently. If you are comparing products, a comparison table is virtually guaranteed to be parsed during a Fan Out process.

Adopt "Answer-First" HTML Structure

Ensure your HTML hierarchy (H1, H2, H3) is logical. Wrap distinct answers in their own sections. The cleaner your code and structure, the easier it is for the AI's retrieval bots to identify your content as a relevant "passage" for a sub-query.

How to Measure Your Brand’s Success in Query Fan Out?

Traditional rank tracking is becoming less reliable. Ranking #1 for a keyword doesn't matter if the AI Overview answers the query without showing your link. You need new metrics.

Tracking Brand Mentions and Share of Voice

Monitor how often your brand or content is cited in AI responses. Use AI visibility tools to monitor your brand’s performance. Analyse the frequency with which an LLM mentions your brand when asked about your industry.

Qualitative Analysis of AI Overviews

Regularly perform manual searches for your high-value keywords. Check the AI Overview.

  • Are you cited?
  • Which part of your content was used?
  • Who are your competitors in the "fanned out" citations?

If you see a competitor cited for "pricing" and you are not, it means their pricing page is better structured for that specific sub-query. 

Visble’s AI visibility tool gives you sentence-wise analysis of every piece of content you track. This not only helps you monitor performance, but also gives you a direction to strategise and improve your brand’s visibility.

Is Your Content Strategy Ready for the Agentive Future?

Query Fan Out is not just a technical tweak. It is a fundamental shift in how human knowledge is retrieved. It moves us away from "searching" and towards "asking."

To succeed, you must stop creating content for search engines and start creating content for reasoning engines (don’t forget it’s a human reading your content, keep the tone and language accordingly). 

If you want your brand to get cited by AI, cover topics with exhaustive depth, structure data for easy extraction, and answer the unasked questions that users imply with their searches.

Excellence in GEO belongs to those who can anticipate the Fan Out. Building comprehensive, entity-rich, and answer-focused content will ensure that no matter how wide the AI casts its net, your brand is the one it catches.

ize":100,"sizes":[]},"width_mobile_extra":{"unit":"%","size":"","sizes":[]},"width_mobile":{"unit":"%","size":"","sizes":[]},"padding_tablet":{"unit":"px","top":"0","right":"0","bottom":"0","left":"0","isLinked":true},"_flex_order_tablet":"end"},"elements":[{"id":"50aa22c","elType":"container","settings":{"boxed_width":{"unit":"px","size":1180,"sizes":[]},"padding":{"unit":"px","top":"40","right":"20","bottom":"50","left":"20","isLinked":false},"content_width":"full","padding_mobile":{"unit":"px","top":"20","right":"20","bottom":"20","left":"20","isLinked":false}},"elements":[{"id":"a3bf606","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

If you are still relying solely on traditional SEO strategies, you are optimising for a version of the internet that is rapidly fading out.<\/span><\/p>

While SEO isn't dead, the rules of visibility have fundamentally changed. To capture attention in 2026, you must adapt to Generative Engine Optimisation (GEO). Recent data underscores this shift: pages optimised for the AI retrieval process are <\/span>161% more likely to be cited in AI Overviews<\/span><\/a>.<\/span><\/p>

At the core of this new ecosystem is a mechanism called Query Fan Out. This guide discusses exactly what Query Fan Out is and provides a blueprint on how to use it to dominate visibility in Google AI Overviews, ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Gemini.<\/span><\/p>

Highlights<\/b><\/h3>
  • Abandon Linear Keywords: <\/b>Focus on user intent and topic clusters rather than single keywords.<\/span><\/li>
  • Optimise for \"Background\" Search:<\/b> Remember that AI Mode runs live queries on Shopping Graphs and Finance data; ensure your schema and data feeds are up-to-date.<\/span><\/li>
  • Structure is Key:<\/b> Use tables, bullet points, and clear headings to help AI parse your data.<\/span><\/li>
  • Own the Data:<\/b> Unique, proprietary data is the best defence against being ignored by AI.<\/span><\/li><\/ul>

    Think in Entities:<\/b> Connect your main topic to all related attributes and concepts to cover the full \"Fan Out\" spectrum.<\/span><\/p>","__globals__":{"link_color":"globals\/colors?id=secondary","link_hover_color":"globals\/colors?id=accent"},"typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Manrope","typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"px","size":35,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":15,"sizes":[]}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"}],"isInner":true},{"id":"eedc401","elType":"container","settings":{"content_width":"full","margin":{"unit":"px","top":"0","right":"0","bottom":"15","left":"0","isLinked":false},"_element_id":"scroll-1"},"elements":[{"id":"1e7945c","elType":"widget","settings":{"title":"What is Query Fan Out in AI Search?","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Plus Jakarta Sans","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":50,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_tablet":{"unit":"px","size":40,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile_extra":{"unit":"px","size":36,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile":{"unit":"px","size":26,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_weight":"600","typography_line_height":{"unit":"em","size":1.2,"sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_laptop":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_tablet_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_mobile_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"text_stroke_text_stroke_type":"yes","_margin":{"unit":"px","top":"0","right":"0","bottom":"20","left":"0","isLinked":false},"_element_width":"inherit","_css_classes":"txt-gradient","_padding_mobile":{"unit":"px","top":"0","right":"20","bottom":"0","left":"20","isLinked":false}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"41e24b9","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

    Traditional SEO gave a list of results to user queries based on keyword matching. Query Fan Out puts an end to this redundant technique. It\u2019s an advanced information retrieval technique used by LLMs and AI search engines.\u00a0<\/span><\/p>

    When a user asks with a complex prompt, the AI does not just search for those specific words. Instead, it \"fans out\" the request, breaking down the single query into multiple, distinct sub-queries (or sub-intents) to gather a complete picture.<\/span><\/p>

    For instance, you search, \"What is the best CRM software for a small real estate business?\"<\/span><\/p>

    • Traditional Search:<\/b> Looks for pages containing keywords like \"best CRM for real estate,\" \"real estate CRM,\" and \"real estate small business.\"<\/span><\/li>
    • AI Search with Query Fan Out:<\/b> The AI reasons that to answer this effectively, it must answer several unasked questions. It splits the main query into sub-queries such as:<\/span>
      • What are the top CRM features for realtors?<\/span><\/li>
      • Price comparison of CRM tools for small teams.<\/span><\/li>
      • Integration capabilities of real estate CRMs with listing sites.<\/span><\/li>
      • User reviews for Salesforce vs. HubSpot for real estate.<\/span><\/li><\/ul><\/li><\/ul>

        The AI executes these searches in parallel, retrieves the data, and synthesises it into a single, cohesive answer (like an AI Overview). If your content answers one of these \"fanned out\" sub-queries, even if you don't rank #1 for the main keyword, you have a high probability of being cited.<\/span><\/p>","__globals__":{"link_color":"globals\/colors?id=primary","link_hover_color":"globals\/colors?id=accent"},"typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Manrope","typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"px","size":35,"sizes":[]},"_padding_mobile":{"unit":"px","top":"0","right":"20","bottom":"0","left":"20","isLinked":false}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"}],"isInner":true},{"id":"74a5e67","elType":"container","settings":{"content_width":"full","margin":{"unit":"px","top":"0","right":"0","bottom":"15","left":"0","isLinked":false},"_element_id":"generativeengine","padding_mobile":{"unit":"px","top":"0","right":"20","bottom":"0","left":"20","isLinked":false}},"elements":[{"id":"cb9ff8f","elType":"widget","settings":{"title":"How Query Fan Out Works in Google\u2019s \"AI Mode\" and Deep Search?","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Plus Jakarta Sans","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":50,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_tablet":{"unit":"px","size":40,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile_extra":{"unit":"px","size":36,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile":{"unit":"px","size":26,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_weight":"600","typography_line_height":{"unit":"em","size":1.2,"sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_laptop":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_tablet_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_mobile_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"text_stroke_text_stroke_type":"yes","_margin":{"unit":"px","top":"20","right":"0","bottom":"20","left":"0","isLinked":false},"_element_width":"inherit","_css_classes":"txt-gradient"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"95f0b45","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

        While Query Fan Out is a general concept in LLMs, it is the specific engine behind Google\u2019s AI Mode. According to recent insights from <\/span>Google\u2019s VP of Product, Robby Stein<\/span><\/a>, AI Mode uses this technique to act as an agent on your behalf.<\/span><\/p>

        When you engage AI Mode or Deep Search, the system doesn't just look for an answer; it conducts a live research session.<\/span><\/p>

        • Real-Time Data Access: <\/b>Unlike standard LLMs that rely on training data, AI Mode fans out queries to real-time systems. It taps into the <\/span>Shopping Graph (which updates 2 billion times per hour)<\/span><\/a> and Google Finance to answer sub-queries about price, stock availability, and market trends instantly.<\/span><\/li>
        • Scale of Execution:<\/b> For complex intents, features like Deep Search can trigger dozens or even hundreds of background queries in seconds.<\/span><\/li>
        • For example, if you search \"Plan a 3-day trip to Goa for a bachelorette party,\" AI Mode fans this out into distinct research paths:<\/span>
          • Restaurants with group seating (checking reservations via Maps data).<\/span><\/li>
          • Live music venues with high safety ratings.<\/span><\/li>
          • Hotels with suites available for specific dates.<\/span><\/li>
          • Weather forecasts for packing tips.<\/span><\/li><\/ul><\/li><\/ul>

            It then aggregates these data points into a single, cohesive itinerary. To be visible here, your content must be accessible to these specific \"background\" searches.<\/span><\/p>","__globals__":{"link_color":"globals\/colors?id=primary","link_hover_color":"globals\/colors?id=accent"},"typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Manrope","typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"px","size":35,"sizes":[]}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"}],"isInner":true},{"id":"28cbf78","elType":"container","settings":{"content_width":"full","margin":{"unit":"px","top":"0","right":"0","bottom":"0","left":"0","isLinked":false},"_element_id":"seovsgeo","padding_mobile":{"unit":"px","top":"0","right":"20","bottom":"0","left":"20","isLinked":false}},"elements":[{"id":"8670f3a","elType":"widget","settings":{"title":"How Does the Query Fan-Out Mechanism Work?","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Plus Jakarta Sans","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":50,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_tablet":{"unit":"px","size":40,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile_extra":{"unit":"px","size":36,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile":{"unit":"px","size":26,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_weight":"600","typography_line_height":{"unit":"em","size":1.2,"sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_laptop":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_tablet_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_mobile_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"text_stroke_text_stroke_type":"yes","_margin":{"unit":"px","top":"20","right":"0","bottom":"20","left":"0","isLinked":false},"_element_width":"inherit","_css_classes":"txt-gradient"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"e61c429","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

            The Query Fan Out mechanism typically operates in four distinct stages within a <\/span>Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)<\/span><\/a> framework.<\/span><\/p>

            1. Intent Decomposition and Reasoning<\/b><\/h3>

            You ask a question, and the LLM starts operating as a reasoning engine. It analyses the semantic intent behind the query with the words or terms used.\u00a0<\/span><\/p>

            It asks: What does the user actually need to know to be satisfied? It identifies entities (e.g., \"CRM,\" \"Real Estate\") and attributes (e.g., \"Price,\" \"Features\") and generates a \"concept map\" of necessary information.<\/span><\/p>

            2. Parallel Sub-Query Execution<\/b><\/h3>

            This is the \"Fan Out\" phase. The system generates 5, 10, or even 20 specific search queries based on the breakdown of the query. These are sent simultaneously to the search index or knowledge graph.\u00a0<\/span><\/p>

            This is why AI search is \"agentive\". It acts as a research assistant performing multiple searches in milliseconds.<\/span><\/p>

            3. Cross-Source Verification and Retrieval<\/b><\/h3>

            The AI retrieves passages, not just whole pages, that answer these specific sub-queries. It prioritises sources with high EEAT (Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, and Trustworthiness) to minimise hallucinations. It looks for consensus across multiple high-authority domains.<\/span><\/p>

            4. Synthesis and Citation<\/b><\/h3>

            Finally, the LLM aggregates the retrieved text chunks. It writes a unique answer for your query, citing the sources that provided the specific data points for each sub-query. Your goal in GEO is to be the source for at least one of these data points.<\/span><\/p>","__globals__":{"link_color":"globals\/colors?id=primary","link_hover_color":"globals\/colors?id=accent"},"typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Manrope","typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"px","size":35,"sizes":[]}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"},{"id":"127fdbe","elType":"container","settings":{"content_width":"full","margin":{"unit":"px","top":"20","right":"0","bottom":"15","left":"0","isLinked":false}},"elements":[{"id":"30b206b","elType":"container","settings":{"content_width":"full","margin":{"unit":"px","top":"0","right":"0","bottom":"15","left":"0","isLinked":false},"_element_id":"correlationgeo","width_mobile":{"unit":"px","size":321.667},"_flex_size":"none","_element_width_mobile":"initial"},"elements":[{"id":"d5ab5a7","elType":"widget","settings":{"title":"What Are the Strategic Benefits and Impacts of Query Fan Out?","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Plus Jakarta Sans","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":50,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_tablet":{"unit":"px","size":40,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile_extra":{"unit":"px","size":36,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile":{"unit":"px","size":26,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_weight":"600","typography_line_height":{"unit":"em","size":1.2,"sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_laptop":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_tablet_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_mobile_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"text_stroke_text_stroke_type":"yes","_margin":{"unit":"px","top":"20","right":"0","bottom":"20","left":"0","isLinked":false},"_element_width":"inherit","_css_classes":"txt-gradient"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"9b0edcf","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

            Shifting your strategy to accommodate Query Fan Out offers distinct competitive advantages that traditional SEO cannot match.<\/span><\/p>

            Bypassing the \"Top 10\" Barrier<\/b><\/h3>

            The most significant impact of Query Fan Out is that you don\u2019t need to be on the first page of SERPs to get cited. About <\/span>68% of citations in AI Overviews<\/span><\/a> come from pages that do not rank in the top 10 for the main query.<\/span><\/p>

            Benefit:<\/b> You no longer need to compete with the giants (like Wikipedia or Amazon) for the main keyword. If you have the best specific answer for a fanned-out sub-query (e.g., specific durability data), the AI must cite you to complete its answer.<\/span><\/p>

            Significantly Increased Citation Odds<\/b><\/h3>

            Optimising for breadth and sub-topics pays off. Pages that rank for fanned-out queries are 161% more likely to be cited in an AI Overview.<\/span><\/p>

            Benefit: <\/b>By covering the \"long tail\" of a topic exhaustively, you triple your chances of appearing in the coveted zero-position, driving high-intent traffic that has already been qualified by the AI.<\/span><\/p>

            Authority Through \"Topic Ownership\"<\/b><\/h3>

            Fan Out queries are volatile; they change based on user context. Chasing every single sub-query is impossible. However, sites that demonstrate Topical Authority, covering a subject so thoroughly that they answer most potential fan-outs, win consistently.<\/span><\/p>

            Benefit:<\/b> More resilient brand presence that dominates the Knowledge Graph, ensuring you are the \"go-to\" entity for the AI.<\/span><\/p>","__globals__":{"link_color":"globals\/colors?id=primary","link_hover_color":"globals\/colors?id=accent"},"typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Manrope","typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"px","size":35,"sizes":[]}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"},{"id":"4b9b7a0","elType":"container","settings":{"content_width":"full","margin":{"unit":"px","top":"0","right":"0","bottom":"15","left":"0","isLinked":false},"_element_id":"musttrackmetrics"},"elements":[{"id":"51b4e30","elType":"widget","settings":{"title":"How Can You Identify Potential Fan Out Opportunities?","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Plus Jakarta Sans","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":50,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_tablet":{"unit":"px","size":40,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile_extra":{"unit":"px","size":36,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile":{"unit":"px","size":26,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_weight":"600","typography_line_height":{"unit":"em","size":1.2,"sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_laptop":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_tablet_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_mobile_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"text_stroke_text_stroke_type":"yes","_margin":{"unit":"px","top":"20","right":"0","bottom":"20","left":"0","isLinked":false},"_element_width":"inherit","_css_classes":"txt-gradient"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"717bfcd","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

            You cannot see the exact sub-queries an AI generates, but you can reverse-engineer them. To optimise for Query Fan Out, you must anticipate the AI's reasoning path.<\/span><\/p>

            1. Map the Entity-Attribute Matrix<\/b><\/h3>

            Stop thinking in keywords and start thinking in Entities. An entity is a distinct concept (person, place, thing, concept). AI understands the world through the relationships between entities and their attributes.<\/span><\/p>

            • Entity: \"iPhone 16\"<\/span><\/li>
            • Attributes: Battery life, Camera specs, Price, Release date, and Colour options.<\/span><\/li><\/ul>

              For every core topic you cover, list every possible attribute a user might care about. The AI will likely fan out queries for each of these attributes.<\/span><\/p>

              2. Leverage \"People Also Ask\" and Auto-Complete<\/b><\/h3>

              Google's \"People Also Ask\" boxes are primitive versions of Query Fan Out. They literally show you the related questions Google associates with a topic. If a People Also Ask question exists, you can bet the AI will fan out a sub-query for it.<\/span><\/p>

              3. Use AI to Simulate Fan Out<\/b><\/h3>

              Use the AI against itself. Input your target keyword into ChatGPT or Gemini and ask: \"Act as a search engine logic bot. Break down the query '[Your Keyword]' into 10 distinct sub-queries you would need to run to provide a comprehensive, expert answer. Focus on user intent and specific data points.\" The output will be your content roadmap.<\/span><\/p>","__globals__":{"link_color":"globals\/colors?id=primary","link_hover_color":"globals\/colors?id=accent"},"typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Manrope","typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"px","size":35,"sizes":[]}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"},{"id":"9416b76","elType":"container","settings":{"content_width":"full","margin":{"unit":"px","top":"20","right":"0","bottom":"15","left":"0","isLinked":false}},"elements":[{"id":"695c8bc","elType":"container","settings":{"content_width":"full","margin":{"unit":"px","top":"0","right":"0","bottom":"0","left":"0","isLinked":false}},"elements":[{"id":"27b492a","elType":"widget","settings":{"title":"How toStructure Content for Maximum AI Readability?","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Plus Jakarta Sans","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":50,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_tablet":{"unit":"px","size":40,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile_extra":{"unit":"px","size":36,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile":{"unit":"px","size":26,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_weight":"600","typography_line_height":{"unit":"em","size":1.2,"sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_laptop":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_tablet_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_mobile_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"text_stroke_text_stroke_type":"yes","_margin":{"unit":"px","top":"20","right":"0","bottom":"20","left":"0","isLinked":false},"_element_width":"inherit","_css_classes":"txt-gradient"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"85b0f19","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

              Generative Engine Optimisation<\/span><\/a> is heavily dependent on formatting. Even if you have the best information, if the AI cannot read it easily, it will ignore it. You need to structure your content so it is crawlable by search engines, along with being readable by humans.\u00a0<\/span><\/p>

              Use the \"Inverted Pyramid\" for Sections<\/b><\/h3>

              For every sub-topic (which corresponds to a sub-query), answer the question directly in the first sentence. Follow with supporting data, and then context. This style allows the AI to easily \"grab\" the answer snippet.<\/span><\/p>

              Implement Explicit Headings and Lists<\/b><\/h3>

              Avoid vague and generic headings written just to add keywords. Use descriptive and entity-rich headings, that too, in question format.<\/span><\/p>

              • Poor Heading:<\/strong> \"Battery Information iPhone 16\"<\/span><\/li>
              • Good Heading:<\/strong> \"How Long Does the iPhone 16 Battery Last?\"<\/span><\/li><\/ul>

                Use bullet points and tables for data. AI models are trained to extract structured data from tables highly efficiently. If you are comparing products, a comparison table is virtually guaranteed to be parsed during a Fan Out process.<\/span><\/p>

                Adopt \"Answer-First\" HTML Structure<\/b><\/h3>

                Ensure your HTML hierarchy (H1, H2, H3) is logical. Wrap distinct answers in their own sections. The cleaner your code and structure, the easier it is for the AI's retrieval bots to identify your content as a relevant \"passage\" for a sub-query.<\/span><\/p>","__globals__":{"link_color":"globals\/colors?id=primary","link_hover_color":"globals\/colors?id=accent"},"typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Manrope","typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"px","size":35,"sizes":[]},"_margin":{"unit":"px","top":"20","right":"0","bottom":"0","left":"0","isLinked":false}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"},{"id":"741e13a","elType":"container","settings":{"content_width":"full","margin":{"unit":"px","top":"0","right":"0","bottom":"30","left":"0","isLinked":false}},"elements":[{"id":"b1da4a8","elType":"widget","settings":{"title":"How to Measure Your Brand\u2019s Success in Query Fan Out?","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Plus Jakarta Sans","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":50,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_tablet":{"unit":"px","size":40,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile_extra":{"unit":"px","size":36,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile":{"unit":"px","size":26,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_weight":"600","typography_line_height":{"unit":"em","size":1.2,"sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_laptop":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_tablet_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_mobile_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"text_stroke_text_stroke_type":"yes","_margin":{"unit":"px","top":"20","right":"0","bottom":"20","left":"0","isLinked":false},"_element_width":"inherit","_css_classes":"txt-gradient"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"2f653bd","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

                Traditional rank tracking is becoming less reliable. Ranking #1 for a keyword doesn't matter if the AI Overview answers the query without showing your link. You need new metrics.<\/span><\/p>

                Tracking Brand Mentions and Share of Voice<\/b><\/h3>

                Monitor how often your brand or content is cited in AI responses. Use<\/span> AI visibility tools<\/span><\/a> to monitor your brand\u2019s performance. Analyse the frequency with which an LLM mentions your brand when asked about your industry.<\/span><\/p>

                Qualitative Analysis of AI Overviews<\/b><\/h3>

                Regularly perform manual searches for your high-value keywords. Check the AI Overview.<\/span><\/p>

                • Are you cited?<\/span><\/li>
                • Which part of your content was used?<\/span><\/li>
                • Who are your competitors in the \"fanned out\" citations?<\/span><\/li><\/ul>

                  If you see a competitor cited for \"pricing\" and you are not, it means their pricing page is better structured for that specific sub-query.\u00a0<\/span><\/p>

                  Visble\u2019s AI visibility tool gives you sentence-wise analysis of every piece of content you track. This not only helps you monitor performance, but also gives you a direction to strategise and improve your brand\u2019s visibility.<\/span><\/p>","__globals__":{"link_color":"globals\/colors?id=primary","link_hover_color":"globals\/colors?id=accent"},"typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Manrope","typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"px","size":35,"sizes":[]}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"},{"id":"a8ef576","elType":"container","settings":{"content_width":"full","margin":{"unit":"px","top":"0","right":"0","bottom":"30","left":"0","isLinked":false}},"elements":[{"id":"9e97f64","elType":"widget","settings":{"title":"Is Your Content Strategy Ready for the Agentive Future?","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Plus Jakarta Sans","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":50,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_tablet":{"unit":"px","size":40,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile_extra":{"unit":"px","size":36,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_size_mobile":{"unit":"px","size":26,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_weight":"600","typography_line_height":{"unit":"em","size":1.2,"sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_laptop":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_tablet_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"typography_line_height_mobile_extra":{"unit":"em","size":"","sizes":[]},"text_stroke_text_stroke_type":"yes","_margin":{"unit":"px","top":"20","right":"0","bottom":"20","left":"0","isLinked":false},"_element_width":"inherit","_css_classes":"txt-gradient"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"f8d8ff9","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

                  Query Fan Out is not just a technical tweak. It is a fundamental shift in how human knowledge is retrieved. It moves us away from \"searching\" and towards \"asking.\"<\/span><\/p>

                  To succeed, you must stop creating content for search engines and start creating content for reasoning engines (don\u2019t forget it\u2019s a human reading your content, keep the tone and language accordingly).\u00a0<\/span><\/p>

                  If you want your <\/span>brand to get cited by AI<\/span><\/a>, cover topics with exhaustive depth, structure data for easy extraction, and answer the unasked questions that users imply with their searches.<\/span><\/p>

                  Excellence in GEO belongs to those who can anticipate the Fan Out. Building comprehensive, entity-rich, and answer-focused content will ensure that no matter how wide the AI casts its net, your brand is the one it catches.<\/span><\/p>","__globals__":{"link_color":"globals\/colors?id=primary","link_hover_color":"globals\/colors?id=accent"},"typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Manrope","typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"px","size":35,"sizes":[]}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"}],"isInner":true}],"isInner":true}],"isInner":true}],"isInner":true}],"isInner":true}],"isInner":true}],"isInner":true}],"isInner":true}],"isInner":true},{"id":"2f5dcf3","elType":"container","settings":{"flex_direction":"row","content_width":"full","width":{"unit":"%","size":25,"sizes":[]},"flex_align_items":"stretch","flex_wrap":"wrap","background_background":"classic","background_image":{"url":"","id":"","size":"","alt":"","source":"library"},"background_position":"center Designation If your SEO strategy still revolves around meta tags and backlinks alone, it’s time for a reality check. You can't play by the old-school tactics anymore since Generative Search Experience is rapidly gaining momentum. ChatGPT reached 365 billion annual searches in just two years, 5.5...
What is Query Fan Out and How to Use it to Improve AI Search Visibility?
Here’s how to use Query Fan Out to dominate AI Search. Learn how LLMs break down intents and use GEO strategies to boost AI Overview citations by 161%.
